body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
<!--
A description of what this pull request does.
-->

Adds OpenGraph tags to the website so it looks more attractive when shared in instant messengers or social media.

More information in the GitHub issue.

You're welcome to suggest changing any particular tag that was set, I just chose what I'd think are sensible values. A better image could be used, but thought I'd leave that one up to you rather, I opted to only pick from what static assets are already being served.

This does do a minor change on the side, if the `title` (tab name) doesn't have `Focalboard` in it, it appends `| Focalboard` to it. (Similar to the behavior you have on the Mattermost website.)

For example, the landing page is still:
`Focalboard: Open source alternative to Trello, Asana, and Notion`

But most other pages went from a format like:
`Personal Edition` → `Personal Edition | Focalboard`

#### Ticket Link
<!--
If this pull request addresses a Help Wanted ticket, please link the relevant GitHub issue, e.g.

  Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server/issues/XXXXX

Otherwise, link the JIRA ticket.
-->

* Closes #3412

#### Previews

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613111-a5b0341a-ef14-4753-899a-1bbb26d724de.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613090-510ad1c9-9282-4607-b60a-5b9dba9b4bef.png)
title: [GH-3412] feat: add opengraph tags to website

triage.summary: Adds OpenGraph meta tags to the Focalboard website so shared links render rich previews in social media and instant messengers. Also appends '| Focalboard' to page titles that don't already include the brand name.
triage.test_hint: Verify OG meta tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url) are present in page source for key pages; check that title fallback appends '| Focalboard' correctly; use a social media debugger tool (e.g. Facebook Sharing Debugger or opengraph.xyz) to validate preview rendering.

body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
There were three Possible Choices to Implement a Mount for a Config File in Docker:

1) docker run -v:
You could specify a Volume at the Start of a Container, but it has the Problem that the Folder on the Container, gets overwritten by the Host. So the Config File would have needed to be Included Explicitly. Additional, it would have needed to be separated from the Other files, because they would have been deleted otherwise in the Process of mounting. 

2) Docker Volume:
Instead of creating it at runtime, it could be created beforehand, which would solve the Above Issue and would Allow for Sharing of Files from the Container with the Host. But this would complicate the Setup needed even further. Therefore, three was chosen. 

3) Dockerfile Volume:
As the focalboard Database already gets its own Volume Created in the Dockerfile to persist, I added a Volume Creation for the Config File as well in the Dockerfile. This requires less Setup, while still allowing for Easy Access of the Config File.

The Config File Source on the Host System can be found via the following Command:
`docker inspect *Container Name* | grep -B 1 "/opt/focalboard" | grep "Source"`
Be Aware that with a WSL 2 Docker Setup under Windows, the Folder Location differs from the one displayed.

(Note: The Container needs to be Restarted for the Updates to take Place)

#### Ticket Link
Provides Solution too https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1889
title: Added Docker Config Volume

triage.skip_reason: Stale PR with UI/infrastructure design decisions; requires human review of Docker volume strategy and security implications of exposing config files via volumes.
triage.summary: Adds a Docker named volume for the Focalboard config file in the Dockerfile, mirroring the existing database volume, to allow easier config file access and persistence.
triage.test_hint: Build the Docker image, run the container, verify config volume is created, modify config via host-side volume mount, restart container and confirm changes are picked up.

At the moment I do see the possibility to archive the entire set of boards I have with `Settings>Export archive`. This gives me an archive of the whole set of boards. 

What if I want to archive just one board or one category that corresponds to a project I completed and so I do not need to see anymore in my Focalboard?

body:
## Summary

Currently database is stored at the /Documents/Focalboard directory in windows, presumably similar in MacOS and Linux. This proposal suggests adding a custom database location.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

I like focalboard as it's a fully portable app, but not being able to have the database in the same dir as the executable makes it more cumbersome to move across devices.

Use cases:
1. Having a focalboard executable and database in a pendrive, so it's fully portable
2. Having a folder with differente databases, being able to tell focalboard at anytime what database to load

## Additional context/similar features

None

Any examples of good implementations of this capability: In the settings menu, add an entry called "Set Database location" that opens a native file explorer dialog and allows to choose an existing .db or a directory where it will be created. This setting is persistent. The focalboard path should be stored in a file next to the executable so focalboard can remain fully portable.
title: Feature Idea: Custom Database location

triage.skip_reason: UI decisions required (native file dialog, settings menu design), cross-platform path handling complexity, and no clear spec for all edge cases (multi-DB switching, migration of existing data).
triage.summary: Allow users to configure a custom database file/directory location via a settings UI, enabling fully portable usage (e.g., running from a USB drive). Config path would be stored next to the executable.
triage.test_hint: Verify custom path is persisted across restarts; test on Windows, macOS, Linux; test with invalid/read-only paths; test migration from default location.

body:
This adds support for displaying inline and displayed Latex equations using Katex in markdown cells, fixing #1509.

The conversion happens before each paragraph is converted from markdown to HTML. At the moment, any parsing error thrown by KaTeX is caught and the chunk ignored. In other words, the following `$\thisisinvalid$` will render as-is, including the surrounding `$`.

# Demonstration
## Markdown editor
![Screenshot from 2021-11-04 11-02-18](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302935-a7862292-e741-4d44-aed6-b8b316f678e7.png)

## Automatic rendering when leaving the editor
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5411875/140302787-e84775a8-d6f2-444d-95e1-572d35762489.png)
title: [GH-1509] Add support for Latex rendering in markdown cells

triage.skip_reason: UI/rendering feature requiring visual validation and dependency addition (KaTeX); needs design/product review and thorough testing of edge cases
triage.summary: Adds KaTeX-based LaTeX rendering support for inline and block math expressions in markdown cells, with graceful fallback on parse errors.
triage.test_hint: Verify inline ($...$) and block ($$...$$) LaTeX renders correctly in markdown cells; confirm invalid LaTeX falls back to raw text; check KaTeX bundle size impact.

body:
#### Summary

This PR adds persistence for the user settings in the native Linux app. The settings are written to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard/settings`. If `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is unset, the app falls back to `$HOME/.config`.

The change hooks into the existing settings export already used in the native macOS app which means the settings are persisted immediately on change.

Unfortunately, the golang webview package uses a custom native binding technology and doesn't allow to define WebKit message handlers. As a result, a dedicated message handler function was added to the existing `NativeApp` object. In the native macOS app, said handler is short-circuited to `window.webkit.messagehandlers.[NAME].postMessage`. This has the benefit that the web app remains agnostic of the particular native binding mechanism.

I have virtually zero experience with Go so while this appears to be working in my testing, the code very likely is an abomination. I'm looking forward to candid feedback. 🙂 

I'd also appreciate some more testing. When running the Linux app on a mac, I _once_ got a blank screen on launch but didn't manage to reproduce again. When testing on Linux, I didn't observe any issues so far.

#### Ticket Link

Relates to https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/314.
title: [GH-314] Persist user settings in native Linux app

triage.skip_reason: Requires native Linux desktop testing, Go webview binding review, and UI/UX validation; intermittent blank screen regression needs investigation before merge
triage.summary: Adds XDG-compliant user settings persistence for the native Linux Focalboard app by hooking into the existing macOS settings export mechanism via a new NativeApp message handler.
triage.test_hint: Launch native Linux app, change user settings, restart app and verify settings are persisted in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/focalboard/settings (or ~/.config/focalboard/settings); also test with XDG_CONFIG_HOME unset; verify no blank screen on launch across multiple restarts on both Linux and macOS.

body:
Summary

Enable a mirroring card feature across multiple boards — when a card is updated on one board, all its mirrored instances on other boards automatically sync and reflect the same changes (title, description, comments, attachments, due dates, etc.).

This would allow teams working across different boards to stay aligned without manual duplication or updates.



How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Keeping cards synchronized across multiple boards is essential for cross-functional collaboration. Currently, duplicating cards and manually updating each one is error-prone and time-consuming. This feature would reduce friction, ensure data consistency, and improve visibility across teams or departments.

Use cases:

Cross-departmental projects: A Product card on the roadmap board is mirrored in the Design and Engineering boards — updates on progress or specs reflect everywhere.

Stakeholder dashboards: A leadership overview board shows key mirrored cards from multiple teams, always up to date.

Templates or shared tasks: Standard operating procedure cards mirrored across projects stay consistent when updated.

Additional context / similar features

Examples of similar implementations:

Notion: Linked database views automatically reflect updates from the original source.

Trello Power-Up “Mirror Cards”: Allows partial mirroring (limited sync capabilities).

ClickUp “Synced Tasks”: Updates to fields sync across mirrored tasks.

Asana “Multi-homing”: One task appears in multiple projects simultaneously.

Future improvements (beyond MVP):

Granular control over what fields are mirrored (e.g., status, due date, or comments).

Sync direction options (one-way vs. two-way).

Visual indicators showing mirrored relationships.

Permissions and visibility control for mirrored cards.
title: Feature Idea: Mirroring Card in different board

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring significant architectural design for cross-board sync, no concrete specs or acceptance criteria, involves backend data model changes, UI decisions, and conflict resolution strategy.
triage.summary: Feature request to support mirroring/syncing cards across multiple boards so that updates to a card (title, description, comments, attachments, due dates) propagate automatically to all mirrored instances on other boards.

body:
**Summary**
A set of enhancements for the "Company Goals & OKRs" template and card functionality to improve usability and flexibility:

1. Allow users to add and delete main Goal cards in the "Company Goals & OKRs" template.

2. Remove or provide an option to disable the automatic emoji assignment for cards.

3. Add rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) to the card description field.

### How important this is to me and why
### Importance: High

### Use cases:

1. Dynamic Goal Management: Companies have more than three goals per quarter/year. The current static template is unusable for real-world planning, forcing users to look for alternatives.

2. Professional Appearance and Clarity: Automatically assigned, irrelevant emojis make boards look unprofessional and chaotic. Manually correcting them is a significant waste of time.

3. Effective Communication: Without text formatting (lists, bold), it is impossible to structure information clearly within a card description, leading to messy and hard-to-read notes.

**Additional context/similar features**
1. Goal Management: Any other task management tool (like Trello, Asana, ClickUp) allows users to create and delete items freely. The inability to do so for the main goals in this template is a critical limitation.

2. Emoji Handling: The best practice is to make emojis optional or allow users to set a default one. Forced automation in this case reduces usability.

3. Text Formatting: This is a standard feature in any modern text editor and most productivity apps (Notion, Trello, Asana). The description field currently feels like a plaintext field from the 1990s.
title: Feature Idea: Enhance Company Goals & OKRs template and card formatting

triage.skip_reason: Multiple distinct feature requests bundled together (template editing, emoji behavior, rich text editor); requires significant UI/UX decisions and architectural work across frontend and possibly backend
triage.summary: Request to enhance Company Goals & OKRs template with: (1) ability to add/delete goal cards freely, (2) option to disable automatic emoji assignment, (3) rich text formatting (bold, lists, underline, strikethrough) in card description field.

body:
## Summary

If you don’t register an account, how can you achieve real-time synchronization and storage of information? Without real-time synchronization, how can you use it for remote collaborative work? If it doesn’t provide real-time network synchronization, there’s a risk that if your computer experiences a sudden system failure one day and doesn’t automatically save, you could lose data, resulting in significant losses. Therefore, having account synchronization functionality and mobile apps is essential. Thank you.

## How important this is to me and why
Real-time synchronization is an essential feature for both individuals and enterprise teams collaborating remotely. Additionally, having mobile apps is equally crucial. 

Those who have the capability should not be restricted to working solely on PCs. For instance, during travel or while on the move, it’s unlikely that one would carry bulky electronic devices like a PC at all times.

Importance: High
title: Feature Idea: Without registering an account, it is impossible to achieve real-time synchronized storage and updates for services like. This lack of security is concerning. I hope that these platforms can also support mobile apps.

triage.skip_reason: Multi-part feature request (account sync, mobile apps) with no concrete specs; requires product/UX decisions and significant engineering effort
triage.summary: User requests account-based real-time sync for data safety and remote collaboration, plus native mobile app support for iOS/Android

body:
**Summary:**
Add a theme editor, where one could define whatever colors they would like for different elements.

When creating new themes there should already be defined usage for different colors, so all different elements uses e.g. "Primary color", "Secondary color" etc. However, if the user would want to override the color for a specific element, it should be possible to do so as well.


**How important this is to me and why:**
Low

Absolutely not essential.
Though, with that said, the first thing I thought when switching to the dark theme was, "Hmm, I wonder if I can easily change the colors". The reason simply being that the colors does not match my perception of a dark theme, but this is of course just personal preference (but therefore the feature request).

Use cases:
1. Create your own themes.
2. Possibility to easily share themes with other users via import/export functionality.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I am not familiar with how the current themes have been implemented, but I assume there is a `.css` file or similar for each theme, so this theme editor would just create additional of these theme files.

For the import/export functionality I am thinking of just having that being a simple `.json` file, having it easily shareable with others and only text. There could even be a text input where one could paste the entire JSON string to import a theme, instead of having to download a file and browse for it.
title: Feature Idea: Theme editor

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, new theme architecture, import/export system, and color management — too broad and open-ended for automated attempt
triage.summary: Add a theme editor allowing users to define custom colors for UI elements using semantic color tokens (primary, secondary, etc.), with per-element overrides and JSON-based import/export for sharing themes.

body:
**Summary:**
Sync contents between application instances.

Maybe this is solved with the server edition, but I understand the server edition more about being able to create multiple users and such. My use case is more along the lines of being a single user, so still only personal use, but being able to use the same database instance on both my desktop computer, multiple laptops etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**
High. For me, this is absolutely essential for this type of application.

Use cases:
1. Being able to switch between computers.
2. Pave the way for mobile apps as requested by issue #160.

**Additional context / similar features:**
I see this could be implemented in a couple of different ways.
- Perhaps storing the `focalboard.db` and `files` folder, as mentioned [here for backups](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/253#issuecomment-822765544), in a synced folder, e.g. Dropbox, OneDrive, Google Drive etc. would be enough?
If so, then simply adding an option in the settings for specifying the location of these files would be enough.
- If the above would not be reliable, e.g. conflicts or locks of having the database open in multiple instances at once, then some check for changes would need to be implemented.
However, if going this route I guess just using the server edition would be the better choice, but that would of course require a server to be always on.
title: Feature Idea: Sync contents between application instances

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring architectural decisions about sync strategy, conflict resolution, and multi-instance coordination; no clear spec and involves UI decisions.
triage.summary: User requests ability to sync Focalboard data (database + files) across multiple desktop instances for a single user, enabling use on multiple computers without a full server setup.

body:
It would be very useful to be able to set a WIP limit (work in progress limit), that enables us to set a limit of how many items can be in one "column" (probably most useful for "doing column", but also for other colums).

This is important since it is one if the central ideas of Kanban board.
See [here](Kanban_boards):
> As described in books on Kanban for software development,[4][3] the two primary practices of Kanban are to visualize your work and limit work in progress (WIP).

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Managing (mental) workload
2. Helps keep task list [SMART](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SMART_criteria) 
3. If used in physical production, helps minimize resources and therefore capital kept "in production"
title: Feature idea: WIP limits

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring UI design decisions, data model changes for per-column WIP limit configuration, and enforcement logic; not suitable for automated attempt
triage.summary: Add WIP (Work In Progress) limit support to Kanban board columns, allowing users to configure a maximum card count per column with visual indicators when the limit is exceeded.
triage.test_hint: Verify that setting a WIP limit on a column shows a warning/indicator when the card count meets or exceeds the limit; verify limit persists across sessions and applies correctly when cards are moved between columns.

1. Paste a print screen in a card for troubleshooting. 
2. Paste print screen in a card for instructions. 

**Additional context / similar features:**
Supported in atom via markdown, they save it in an asset folder and add it via markdown in the context.

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring clipboard API integration, file handling, and storage changes; no clear spec for implementation details
triage.summary: Allow users to paste images directly from clipboard onto a card, avoiding the need to save and upload temp files manually.

body:
**Summary:**
Keep track of deleted content.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. It's easy to mis-click sometimes, and once a card is deleted, it would be good to be able to restore it.
2. Currently there's no backup (#253) so there's no way to restore from there
3. Currently exporting is broken on the personal edition (#248) so that's one less option for the previous point (I guess that leaves making frequent copies of the .db file and digging in with SQLite if one needs to restore an individual card?)

**Additional context / similar features:**
Trello has this with its "archive" feature.  Asana has something similar.

(It would make sense that the import scripts make use of this once this is implemented.  Currently, the Trello import script takes all cards that were archived in Trello the same as if they were not archived.)
title: Feature Idea: Recycle bin / archive individual cards

triage.skip_reason: Large feature requiring backend soft-delete logic, UI for recycle bin/archive view, restore workflows, and potential migration; too broad for automated attempt.
triage.summary: Add recycle bin / archive support so deleted cards can be restored instead of being permanently removed, similar to Trello's archive feature.

body:
**Summary:**
I know markdown supports checklists but I would want to be able to sort tasks in a better way. Say we have a project that consists of stages planning, building, testing, reporting, lessons learned and that planning is made up of brainstorming, selecting ideas, scheduling and reporting is built of writing draft, filling in missing information, fixing appearance, fixing references, proof reading, editing, publishing. How would I sort this in a way so that not everything is just added in a long list of tasks? Maybe by adding a property and sort by it? When working in larger projects this would be very useful.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium
This would make the product much more useful, but it might be hard to implement in a good way. One can find work arounds but a native solution would be very nice.

Use cases:
1. Divide large projects into smaller parts to get a better understanding of progress.
title: Feature Idea: Subtasks

triage.skip_reason: Subtasks require significant architectural changes to the data model, UI hierarchy, and filtering/sorting logic; no concrete spec provided and involves major UI decisions.
triage.summary: Feature request to support hierarchical subtasks, allowing tasks to be nested under parent tasks for better project organization rather than a flat list.

body:
**Summary:**

This app could be really nice to use with something like emacs org mode or markdown files. This makes using this program in a serverless setup (e.g. via syncthing) very easy and would make it ultimately even possible to have a scrum board checked in with the source code for colaboration. 

Having a slim and content (Blocks and Comments/Atachments) oriented (Go) Interface in order to make it easier to add a new storage  backend would certainly be a good first step. A generalised storage backend plugin could use shell scripts as adaptors for reading file based storage backends (converting eg. between org-mode and json two ways). 

**How important this is to me and why:**

I personally have been looking for an application like this for a long time but keep all my textural data file based and searcheable with grep. If I could make this application could fit into my existing file based notes, dates workflow I'd  start using it immediately. 

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Accessing and editing the board both from IDE and Focalboard app
2. Serverless Collaboration between people via SMB / Dropbox / Syncthing folders.
3. Generally easier integration into existing development ecosystems and pipelines.  

**Additional context / similar features:**

While using one file-per-board, file-per-lane or even file-per-block would certainly improve the problem of edit collisions (as compared to project-per-sqlite-db ) when working on a shared storage it doesn't completely solve the problem of conflicts. This situation has been addressed by: https://github.com/39aldo39/DecSync 

I offer to help with the implementation of such a feature If it finds wider appreciation.
title: Feature Request: File based storage backend

triage.skip_reason: Large architectural feature requiring a new storage backend abstraction layer, file format design decisions, and conflict resolution strategy. No clear spec, involves significant design choices.
triage.summary: Request for a file-based storage backend (e.g., markdown/org-mode files) to enable serverless collaboration via Syncthing/Dropbox/SMB and integration with file-based workflows. Would require a generalized storage backend interface in Go.

body:
Right now, we have [scripts to import basic info](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/tree/main/import) from popular systems like Trello, Asana, and Notion. They can pull in the card titles, descriptions, and basic properties from a single board. Expand these further to do things like:
* Import advanced properties (e.g. dates, text, numbers)
* Handle multiple boards
* Handle Trello PowerUp properties
* Handle sub-tasks in Asana
* Use the [Notion API](https://www.notion.so/api-beta) to import rich data
* Import from other popular systems

This high-level issue can be split into smaller ones to handle targeted improvements
title: Extend import scripts

triage.skip_reason: Large feature with multiple sub-tasks, requires human breakdown and design decisions
triage.summary: Extend import scripts to support advanced properties (dates, text, numbers), multiple boards, Trello PowerUps, Asana sub-tasks, Notion API, and additional systems.

body:
## Context

Earlier this week Trello announced [new collaborator limits](https://www.atlassian.com/blog/trello/new-collaborator-limits) on Free workspaces, with 10 members being the cut-off limit for the Free plan.

This change is disrupting the work pipelines in some of the non-profit projects I am involved with, and I am looking for alternatives.
It's not just me; there are a few popular topics on Reddit from people who've faced the same problem ([tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/selfhosted/comments/1b85rq3/alternative_to_trello/), [tap](https://www.reddit.com/r/trello/comments/1b7g4sm/completely_free_alternatives_to_trello/)).

Focalboard keeps popping up -- followed by a note about "but it's discontinued, so.."
Either way, currently this repo attracts attention from folks considering to use or self-host a Trello alternative.

## Opportunity

This could be an opportunity to bootstrap a community effort around Focalboard.
The crowd might big enough to run an "official community fork" and start working on it.
Otherwise the effort could be duplicated, or people will move on to other projects.

## Call for action

Please comment if you're willing to contribute. 
Please do mention areas you'd like to support (i.e. front-end, back-end, UX etc) and anything else you consider relevant.

## Personal story

IMO Focalboard has a friendly codebase which is easy to navigate.
I don't have much experience with Typescript or Go. Still, I was able to enhance the Trello importer with some medium effort (#4874, not converted into a PR yet), adding import support for custom fields etc.
title: Trello is enforcing more limits, could this be an opportunity for Focalboard? Official community fork?

triage.skip_reason: Community/governance discussion, not a concrete technical issue; requires human decision-making about project direction and forking strategy
triage.summary: User requests an official community fork of Focalboard in response to Trello's new free-tier collaborator limits driving migration interest

body:
> We promise that we will not enforce the copyleft provisions in AGPL v3.0 against you if your application (a) does not
link to Focalboard directly, but exclusively uses Focalboard's Admin Tools and Configuration Files, and
(b) you have not modified, added to or adapted the source code of Focalboard in a way that results in the creation of
a “modified version” or “work based on” Focalboard as these terms are defined in the AGPL v3.0 license.

If I take some portions of the backend code and modify it to produce an application that is `not similar` to Focalboard and/or Mattermost, for example use this code to create a B2B application. Is it allowed to do so?

Thanks
title: Requesting clarification in your license

triage.skip_reason: Legal/licensing clarification requires human judgment from maintainers or legal counsel; cannot be auto-resolved
triage.summary: User requesting clarification on whether modifying Focalboard backend code to build a dissimilar B2B application is permitted under the AGPL v3.0 license exception clause.

Updates `actions/upload-artifact` from 4.6.2 to 7.0.0
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v7.0.0

v7 What's new

Direct Uploads

Adds support for uploading single files directly (unzipped). Callers can set the new archive parameter to false to skip zipping the file during upload. Right now, we only support single files. The action will fail if the glob passed resolves to multiple files. The name parameter is also ignored with this setting. Instead, the name of the artifact will be the name of the uploaded file.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Add proxy integration test by @​Link- in actions/upload-artifact#754
  • Upgrade the module to ESM and bump dependencies by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#762
  • Support direct file uploads by @​danwkennedy in actions/upload-artifact#764

New Contributors

  • @​Link- made their first contribution in actions/upload-artifact#754

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/upload-artifact/compare/v6...v7.0.0

v6.0.0

v6 - What's new

[!IMPORTANT] actions/upload-artifact@v6 now runs on Node.js 24 (runs.using: node24) and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1. If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

Node.js 24

This release updates the runtime to Node.js 24. v5 had preliminary support for Node.js 24, however this action was by default still running on Node.js 20. Now this action by default will run on Node.js 24.

What's Changed

  • Upload Artifact Node 24 support by @​salmanmkc in actions/upload-artifact#719

Updates `github/codeql-action` from 3.28.19 to 4.35.1
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v4.35.1

  • Fix incorrect minimum required Git version for improved incremental analysis: it should have been 2.36.0, not 2.11.0. #3781

v4.35.0

  • Reduced the minimum Git version required for improved incremental analysis from 2.38.0 to 2.11.0. #3767
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.1. #3773

v4.34.1

  • Downgrade default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3 due to issues with a small percentage of Actions and JavaScript analyses. #3762

v4.34.0

  • Added an experimental change which disables TRAP caching when improved incremental analysis is enabled, since improved incremental analysis supersedes TRAP caching. This will improve performance and reduce Actions cache usage. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3569
  • We are rolling out improved incremental analysis to C/C++ analyses that use build mode none. We expect this rollout to be complete by the end of April 2026. #3584
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.0. #3585

v4.33.0

  • Upcoming change: Starting April 2026, the CodeQL Action will skip collecting file coverage information on pull requests to improve analysis performance. File coverage information will still be computed on non-PR analyses. Pull request analyses will log a warning about this upcoming change. #3562

    To opt out of this change:

    • Repositories owned by an organization: Create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-file-coverage-on-prs and the type "True/false", then set this property to true in the repository's settings. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. Alternatively, if you are using an advanced setup workflow, you can set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using default setup: Switch to an advanced setup workflow and set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using advanced setup: Set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.

  • Fixed a bug which caused the CodeQL Action to fail loading repository properties if a "Multi select" repository property was configured for the repository. #3557

  • The CodeQL Action now loads custom repository properties on GitHub Enterprise Server, enabling the customization of features such as github-codeql-disable-overlay that was previously only available on GitHub.com. #3559

  • Once private package registries can be configured with OIDC-based authentication for organizations, the CodeQL Action will now be able to accept such configurations. #3563

  • Fixed the retry mechanism for database uploads. Previously this would fail with the error "Response body object should not be disturbed or locked". #3564

  • A warning is now emitted if the CodeQL Action detects a repository property whose name suggests that it relates to the CodeQL Action, but which is not one of the properties recognised by the current version of the CodeQL Action. #3570

v4.32.6

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3. #3548

v4.32.5

  • Repositories owned by an organization can now set up the github-codeql-disable-overlay custom repository property to disable improved incremental analysis for CodeQL. First, create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-disable-overlay and the type "True/false" in the organization's settings. Then in the repository's settings, set this property to true to disable improved incremental analysis. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. This feature is not yet available on GitHub Enterprise Server. #3507
  • Added an experimental change so that when improved incremental analysis fails on a runner — potentially due to insufficient disk space — the failure is recorded in the Actions cache so that subsequent runs will automatically skip improved incremental analysis until something changes (e.g. a larger runner is provisioned or a new CodeQL version is released). We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3487
  • The minimum memory check for improved incremental analysis is now skipped for CodeQL 2.24.3 and later, which has reduced peak RAM usage. #3515
  • Reduced log levels for best-effort private package registry connection check failures to reduce noise from workflow annotations. #3516
  • Added an experimental change which lowers the minimum disk space requirement for improved incremental analysis, enabling it to run on standard GitHub Actions runners. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3498
  • Added an experimental change which allows the start-proxy action to resolve the CodeQL CLI version from feature flags instead of using the linked CLI bundle version. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3512
  • The previously experimental changes from versions 4.32.3, 4.32.4, 3.32.3 and 3.32.4 are now enabled by default. #3503, #3504

v4.32.4

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.2. #3493
  • Added an experimental change which improves how certificates are generated for the authentication proxy that is used by the CodeQL Action in Default Setup when private package registries are configured. This is expected to generate more widely compatible certificates and should have no impact on analyses which are working correctly already. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in February. #3473
  • When the CodeQL Action is run with debugging enabled in Default Setup and private package registries are configured, the "Setup proxy for registries" step will output additional diagnostic information that can be used for troubleshooting. #3486
  • Added a setting which allows the CodeQL Action to enable network debugging for Java programs. This will help GitHub staff support customers with troubleshooting issues in GitHub-managed CodeQL workflows, such as Default Setup. This setting can only be enabled by GitHub staff. #3485
  • Added a setting which enables GitHub-managed workflows, such as Default Setup, to use a nightly CodeQL CLI release instead of the latest, stable release that is used by default. This will help GitHub staff support customers whose analyses for a given repository or organization require early access to a change in an upcoming CodeQL CLI release. This setting can only be enabled by GitHub staff. #3484

v4.32.3

  • Added experimental support for testing connections to private package registries. This feature is not currently enabled for any analysis. In the future, it may be enabled by default for Default Setup. #3466

... (truncated)

Updates `microsoft/setup-msbuild` from 1.3 to 3
Release notes

Sourced from microsoft/setup-msbuild's releases.

v3 Update to move to Node24

What's Changed

  • Fix small typo in README.md by @​GeckoEidechse in microsoft/setup-msbuild#129
  • Update to Node24 by @​timheuer in microsoft/setup-msbuild#145

New Contributors

  • @​GeckoEidechse made their first contribution in microsoft/setup-msbuild#129

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v2...v3

v2

This is a major version bump with no functionality changes. The major version change is due to moving to node20 for the node version per the deprecation announcement for GitHub Actions (https://github.blog/changelog/2023-09-22-github-actions-transitioning-from-node-16-to-node-20/). This change from node16 to node20 could cause breaking changes to other workflow actions steps (see #125) running and needing/leveraging expected functionality in node16 and thus as a safe boundary, changing the version here.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v1.3...v2

v1.3.3

This is a patch update that reverts the node16->node20 change which introduced unintended breaking changes for users of this action. The v1, v1.3 tags have been updated to this release as well.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/microsoft/setup-msbuild/compare/v1.3.2...v1.3.3

body:
Hi team,

I wonder if the September 15th, 2023 move to "fully community supported" model and the "We encourage the community to fork this repository for continued development and contributions." statement warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

Currently it [outlines a mix of MIT, GNU AGPL v.3.0 and commercial license](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt), as follows:

```
You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE

-	See MIT-COMPILED-LICENSE.md included in compiled versions for details

You may be licensed to use source code to create compiled versions not produced by Mattermost, Inc. in one of two ways:

1. Under the Free Software Foundation’s GNU AGPL v.3.0, subject to the exceptions outlined in this policy; or
2. Under a commercial license available from Mattermost, Inc. by contacting commercial@mattermost.com

You are licensed to use the source code in Admin Tools and Configuration Files (webapp/html-templates/, app-config.json,
config.json, webapp/i18n/, server/model/, plugin/ and all subdirectories thereof) under the Apache License v2.0.

We promise that we will not enforce the copyleft provisions in AGPL v3.0 against you if your application (a) does not
link to Focalboard directly, but exclusively uses Focalboard's Admin Tools and Configuration Files, and
(b) you have not modified, added to or adapted the source code of Focalboard in a way that results in the creation of
a “modified version” or “work based on” Focalboard as these terms are defined in the AGPL v3.0 license.
```

I am not sure to what extent it reflects the "community supported" model. It sounds like patched and self-hosted deployments may need to be careful in using the Focalboard name / logo and/or any © signs if they are displayed somewhere in the app(s).

Any advice?

P.S. I am a developer, not a lawyer.
title: Does "fully community supported" warrant an update of LICENSE.txt?

triage.skip_reason: Legal/licensing decision requires human judgment from Mattermost legal team; not a code change that can be automated
triage.summary: License.txt may need updating to reflect the September 2023 transition to a fully community-supported model, as the current text still references commercial licensing, Mattermost-produced compiled versions, and AGPL enforcement policies that may no longer apply.

body:
**Summary:**
Set a date for regular releases, e.g. 16th of the month (to match Mattermost) and 1st of the month (because we can ship faster as an early project) 

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Deliver features to community faster, so community gets more value, and we can get more feedback, iterate, and demonstrate project velocity 
2. Our [changelog](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#work-in-progress---v07-release---date-tbd) isn't overflowing by the time of next release. 
3. We encourage ourselves to think about upgrades, at least to Personal Desktop Edition 
4. Build excitement, anticipation and awareness among community 

**Additional context / similar features:**
Watching the changelog grow and not being able to get the new improvements and features is like having our noses pressed up against a glass wall, looking at amazing software we can see but can't yet use.
title: Feature Idea: Release cadence

triage.skip_reason: Process/release cadence decision requires human judgment and team coordination, not a code change
triage.summary: Establish a regular release cadence (e.g. 1st and 16th of each month) to deliver features faster, align with Mattermost release schedule, and build community engagement.

body:
## Summary

I often find myself searching for a couple of different cards in quick succession and a constant friction that I notice is having to remove the existing text when I do an alternate search. It would be nice if the search box had a clear button, that I could click to both bring focus to the search box and clear the existing search query. Here's an example of my suggestion/request:

Currently:
![screenshot](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/84977e19-d1f6-4c1d-a281-1eb368458015)

With a search clear button:
![screenshot-clear](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/assets/818633/3b08c5b1-be43-4300-87f3-3a9e97e90cbb)

Obviously there are workarounds e.g. `<Ctrl>`-`<a>` to select all text, then start typing (when focus is already in the search bar), or if focus is elsewhere, then `<Ctrl>`-`<f>` will achieve the same ends. But this inconsistency is part of my friction. I can't just do a consistent single action to clear the search box.

Another workaround with the mouse is to select all search text and start typing, but that involves getting the selection right, which requires more thought and/or mouse accuracy than say, clicking a button :grimacing: .

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium to Low (annoyance)

## Additional context/similar features

IME a search "clear" button is common and almost ubiquitous, anywhere a search box is found. E.g. google search.

I suspect that the implementation would be relatively trivial and I'd potentially be open to having a go myself. However, I don't really know go or typescript/javascript (beyond the very basic basics - and I suspect the latter would be required, possibly the former too?). I'm familiar with a number of other languages, so I'm sure I could fudge my way through it and at least string together a PoC. But without some pointers on where I should start, it feels pretty overwhelming...

Plus there's also the issue that even if I do the work and implement this myself, now focalboard is "community supported", it's unclear when or even if it would get merged?!
title: Feature Idea: Search box "clear" button

body:
## Summary

For the filters section, instead of asking for a fixed date or fixed person, make it customized to the time period or whoever is logged in. 

This allows for pages of boards to be created to view outstanding issues due in a short period of time and triage tasks relevant to the person that is looking at the board 

## How important this is to me and why
There are 3 use cases that I can outline. In my view, this feature would greatly reduce confusion and increase the usability of the boards. Because of my current project setup, the number of people and the tasks on the board are too difficult to consume. Not everyone is well versed in changing the filtering to their liking, and once they do, it changes for everyone. Creating views per person is not practical and the boards are unusable without selective filtering. 

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Filter down large boards to cards relevant to the user 
2. Create dynamic views based on date 
3. When dynamic filters are built, this will allow filters to be present for all users
 

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: Dynamic Filters

body:
## Summary

Emphasizing the use of websockets in the Docker Personal Server documentation could prevent issues in the future.

## Link to documentation page

[Docket setup guide](https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/)

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.

I am a bit new to self hosting, and am running the Personal Server with NGINX Proxy Manager. I didn't enable websockets support which [caused issues](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/2183#issuecomment-1831272394).

The Ubuntu setup guide mentions that websockets are used, but those setting up with Docker would likely not see this.

body:
## Summary

This feature is about being able to set a date and time to automatically change a card field.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. We are using cards for meetings and to do, and sometimes we decide to move it to the next meeting, or to a specified time. It would be awesome to be able to change the "meeting status" field automatically.
2. On a To Do board, some actions require to wait a specific period of time before being able to do it, and move it from "waiting" to "to do" automatically would be a must.

## Additional context/similar features

body:
I'm pretty sure that this is a shortcoming of the current documentation<s>, although there is a chance that it's a bug</s>.

I'm using Focalboard as a Mattermost plugin and have just recently started playing with the API.

It took me a while to get it going as the docs are a bit suboptimal (I want to complain, but I work in open source too and I get that there are only so many hours in the day).

FWIW these 2 issues are essential reading for getting started with the Focalboard Mattermost plugin API IMO:
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1116
- https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4420

Plus obviously the actual API docs:
https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/server/swagger/docs/html/index.html#api-Default-getCards

On face value it all appeared to be working well. Then I noticed that there were some cards missing! When I dug in a little deeper, I realised that it was only returning 100 results<s>?!</s>.

I note that the docs (at least for `cards`) show the suffixes `?page=56&per_page=56`

So I tried checking with `?page=1` and that shows <s>one of</s> the missing cards, so on the right track. Then I tried with `?page=2` and got no results<s>?!</s> I also tried with `?page=0` and that just gave me the same 100 results I was getting initially (so <s>I assume that</s> **with no suffix it defaults to `?page=0&per_page=100`**!<s>?</s>).

After a bit of trial and error, I ended up getting all the cards via a `?page=0&per_page=200` suffix (I have less than 200 cards). So whilst I'm now unstuck, I do have some remaining questions:

- Why does `?page=2` give no results?! Shouldn't whatever doesn't fit on page 1 spill over to page 2 and so on?
  - To answer my own question: Yes. `page=0` is the first page of results, `page=1` is the second page, `page=2` returns no results as I have less than 200 cards!
- <s>How do I ensure that I'm getting all cards (without just increasing `per_page` until it gives no extra results)? Is there a way to page through all results?</s>
- And/or is there a way to force the API to return all results (i.e. turn off pagination)?

body:
## Summary

Hello, and thank you for amazing project!
There is one feature missing that is blocking me to start using it:


Sub-groups, the same way as notion do:
![picture](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/7556217/be0c1478-0ef8-4ce3-b83f-40884ab57cbb)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
Personally I use boards to track processes in my team.
I have a single board with my team members and category of task:

```
           +-------+-------+-------+
           |member1|member2|member3|
    Inbox  +=======+=======+=======+
           | task4 |       |       |
Unplanned  +-------+-------+-------+
           |       |       | task5 |
           |       |       | task7 |
     ASAP  +-------+-------+-------+
           | task1 | task2 |       |
           |       | task3 |       |
  Planned  +-------+-------+-------+
           | task8 |       | task6 |
           | task9 |       |       |
           | task0 |       |       |
           +-------+-------+-------+
```

We have daily meetings to track the process for each task

## Additional context/similar features

Read the notion announce: 
- https://www.notion.so/releases/2021-10-19

Thanks!
title: Feature Idea: sub-groups

body:
## Summary

I wanted to install Focalboard on my Oracle Cloud Free Tier VM (which has an arm64 cpu) to use it with my team but in the tags information on Docker Hub I've seen that the provided docker image is just for amd64 architectures.
However, from Issue #124 of this repository I understand that now also ARM64 architectures are supported.
Therefore, I would like to know how could I spin up Focalboard on an arm machine and, since this kind of machines is getting quite popular among servers, I think it would be useful to add those informations to the install instructions of the project (which at the moment are not that exhaustive, for example not reporting how to have persistent storage of Focalboard container data either)

## Link to documentation page
https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/docker/
title: Doc: Add documentation on how to run Focalboard with docker (or docker-compose) on ARM64 machines

body:
## Summary

When working with a Personal Desktop on windows I see a Port listed in my application window header. I have confirmed that if I go to my http://localhost:{port} I do reach a login page for focalboard but cannot create a user or sign in with any default credentials that I can find. I see in the files unzipped from the raw download there is a config.json that has port 8088 defined which differs from the port in the header and is also non-functioning by default. What is the purpose of that file?

What I would like to do is have the ability to load the tool on a machine and share the webview URL where the board could be reviewed perhaps over a team meeting live to make updates. This ideal scenario would be better if it were possible to use local tag-like Assignees instead of different account holders if its not possible to create accounts on the local/webview interfaces. 

If this is not possible; could some comment or documentation be added regarding the webview's access from the personal desktop application and its non-functionality? Something that if someone else discovers in the future they can confirm is desired behavior vs a bug.

As a final note: I am unable to use the server edition of your software as only the personal desktop is accessible FOSS for internal usage. 

## Link to documentation page
N/A

## (Optional) Additional context and/or screenshot

Add additional context and/or a screenshot of the product feature you'd like explained in documentation.

body:
## Summary

I am currently in the process of moving from Asana to Focalboard. One feature that I know I'm going to miss is the custom rule sets and automation. One key missing feature is the moving tasks (or change status) based on the date. If the Due Date is coming up, there should be an option that allows you to change the status.

## How important this is to me and why
This will allow you to use this tool more of a to-do list as well. It will effectively force you to look at the task as it will move the task in front of your eyes.

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. I know I need to do Errand A on Monday, When Monday comes, it would be great if this task will go to the top of my table.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Install the latest focalboard with the guides provided in https://www.focalboard.com/download/personal-edition/ubuntu/
  - installed with postgresql
  - focalboard v7.11.3 fresh installation
2. Create account, create board, ...
3. Add any file on `Attachment` on board
4. Delete file on `Attachment`

## Expected behavior

File needs to be deleted and queries need to be executed as expected.

## Current behavior

File does not get deleted. Cards with attachments will never be deleted.

The log show that the ```relation `fileinfo`  does not exist.```.

```sh
$ systemctl status focalboard.service
...
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.394 +09:00] Broadcast block change                   caller="ws/server.go:554" teamID=0 blockID=(block_id) remoteAddr="[::1]:38372"
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.394 +09:00] Broadcast block change                   caller="ws/server.go:554" teamID=0 blockID=(block_id) remoteAddr="[::1]:37072"
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.395 +09:00] POST Blocks                              caller="api/blocks.go:318" block_count=1 disable_notify=false
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.398 +09:00] Block change event                       caller="notifylogger/logger_backend.go:48" action=add board="board" card="test" block_id=(block_id)
Sep 29 00:16:43 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:16:43.522 +09:00] attachSession                            caller="api/auth.go:333" single_user=false
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: debug [2023-09-29 00:17:21.121 +09:00] attachSession                            caller="api/auth.go:333" single_user=false
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: error [2023-09-29 00:17:21.148 +09:00] pq: relation "fileinfo" does not exist   caller="api/api.go:183"
Sep 29 00:17:21 apps focalboard-server[65981]: error [2023-09-29 00:17:21.148 +09:00] API ERROR                                caller="api/api.go:200" code=500 error="pq: relation "fileinfo" does not exist" api=/api/v2/boards/(board_name)/blocks/(block_id)
```

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: v7.11.3
 - Browser and OS: Debian Bookworm

## Additional context

I think the focalboard was using `fileinfo` relation in the past. but now it seems to be replaced to `file_info`. (Correct me if I'm wrong)
I don't really know why `fileinfo` is still used when `file_info` exists.

psql log relation list

```sh
$ sudo --login --user postgres
postgres@apps:~$ psql
psql (15.3 (Debian 15.3-0+deb12u1))

# \dt
                  List of relations
 Schema |         Name          | Type  |   Owner
--------+-----------------------+-------+------------
 public | blocks                | table | focalboard
 public | blocks_history        | table | focalboard
 public | board_members         | table | focalboard
 public | board_members_history | table | focalboard
 public | boards                | table | focalboard
 public | boards_history        | table | focalboard
 public | categories            | table | focalboard
 public | category_boards       | table | focalboard
 public | db_lock               | table | focalboard
 public | file_info             | table | focalboard
 public | notification_hints    | table | focalboard
 public | preferences           | table | focalboard
 public | schema_migrations     | table | focalboard
 public | sessions              | table | focalboard
 public | sharing               | table | focalboard
 public | subscriptions         | table | focalboard
 public | system_settings       | table | focalboard
 public | teams                 | table | focalboard
 public | users                 | table | focalboard
(19 rows)
```

Thank you for your time & consideration.

## Summary

Enhance the Trello importer to support more fields.
Currently the `importTrello.ts` script only imports card name, list, description and subtasks (if any).

It would be nice to support import for more Trello fields (labels, comments, custom fields, ...).

I might be happy to work on the PR, if maintainers are ok with this enhancement.

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to Mattermost Boards.
2. Open any board where you have "Team Member" permissions.
3. Start a new comment in any card.
4. Try to mention a user using the "@" symbol.
5. See error: Only a subset of users is displayed in the autocomplete dropdown.

## Expected behavior

When using the "@" symbol to mention another user in a comment, all available users should be displayed in the autocomplete dropdown for users with "Team Member" permissions, similar to how it works for "Team Administrators."

 - Mattermost Version: 7.8.6 (docker image mattermost/mattermost-enterprise-edition free plan)

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

- I'm running stand-alone Focalboard 7.10.4 in Docker (left screenshot)
- I spun up a Mattermost instance in Docker with Boards 7.11.2 (right screenshot)
- I exported my board from Focalboard to a `boardarchive` file
- When importing the `boardarchive` to Mattermost, all of the timestamps are wrong
- Also, the order of the items in the columns is lost (unsure if this is related to the timestamp changes)

Below you can see the left (Focalboard) has the correct time of 10 months ago, while the right (Mattermost import) has the incorrect time of 12 days ago
![Screenshot_20230829_115834](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/52bb70e5-7724-4423-9202-bfc4ba26abc0)

Here is the JSON from the Focalboard export for that entry.
```
{"type":"block","data":{"id":"ayiaef857sjy1tb5psu3hkp9pka","parentId":"cukrxnfw8n3dmjnbgjc1n3uz3wa","createdBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","modifiedBy":"u1b6q77ixji8mtg84agcfbfp6ew","schema":1,"type":"comment","title":"I think this is done, but Ansible playbooks will need to be redone to ensure it's done for the correct user","fields":{},"createAt":1667328548338,"updateAt":1667328548338,"deleteAt":0,"boardId":"bfy769tekmtgw9gcuzc8b5cqsne"}}
```

You can see the timestamp is `1667328548338`, which was 10 months ago (the correct time). I'm unsure how Mattermost is getting 12 days ago...
![Screenshot_20230829_120249](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/be1cca08-4b67-4c92-a29d-758fa965777b)

Here is a different example where the time on the import was updated to the current time...

![Screenshot_20230829_120757](https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/assets/4955337/ce94ab9a-defd-4ffc-8cb5-0f0fdf62fa6b)

body:
After updating Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or from 7.8.2 to 7.8.9 (ESR) the following error occurs on startup. Running `journalctl -u mattermost -r` gives the following output:
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.498 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Failed to sync plugin from file store","caller":"app/plugin.go:366","bundle":"plugins/focalboard.tar.gz","error":"installExtractedPlugin: Unable to restart plugin on upgrade., error initializing the DB: Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1"}
> 
> Aug 20 14:21:54 mattermost mattermost[1370]: {"timestamp":"2023-08-20 14:21:54.478 +02:00","level":"error","msg":"Table creation / migration failed","caller":"app/plugin_api.go:976","plugin_id":"focalboard","error":"\"Error 1054: Unknown column 'dirty' in 'field list' in line 0: SELECT version, dirty FROM `focalboard_schema_migrations` LIMIT 1\""}


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

Update Mattermost (Team Edition) from 7.1.5 (ESR) to 7.8.2 (ESR) or 7.8.9 (ESR):
```
sudo su
systemctl stop mattermost

# Backup
cd /opt
rm -rf mattermost-back* mattermost-upgrade
cp -ra mattermost/ mattermost-back-$(date +'%F-%H-%M')/

# Upgrade
wget https://releases.mattermost.com/7.8.9/mattermost-team-7.8.9-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar -xf mattermost*.gz --transform='s,^[^/]\+,\0-upgrade,'

find mattermost/ mattermost/client/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 \! \( -type d \( -path mattermost/client -o -path mattermost/client/plugins -o -path mattermost/config -o -path mattermost/logs -o -path mattermost/plugins -o -path mattermost/data \) -prune \) | sort | sudo xargs rm -r

cp -an mattermost-upgrade/. mattermost/
chown -R mattermost:mattermost mattermost
setcap cap_net_bind_service=+ep ./mattermost/bin/mattermost

# Restart service
systemctl start mattermost

# View log
journalctl -u mattermost -r
```

## Expected behavior
Mattermost should start without errors.

## Edition and Platform
 - Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.8.2
 - Server OS: Ubuntu 20.04

body:
[Copied from https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/24148]

#### Summary
Changed date property not reflected in board card

#### Steps to reproduce
1. Create a board.
2. Open a new card for editing.
3. Add a custom date property to the card (and therefore to all cards on the board).
4. Set the value of the custom date property on the new card and close it.
5. Click "Properties" and enable the display of the new property on the board.
6. Open the card back up, change the value of the date property, and close the card.

#### Expected behavior
I expect to see the new value I just entered for the date property to be displayed on the card on the board display.

#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 
The old value of the date field is still displayed on the card on the board display.

The two ways I've found to get the new value to display are:

* quit and restart the app
* disable and re-enable the display of the date property on the board then the displayed value is updated

### But wait there's more

Two other incorrect behaviors I'm observing with date properties which there's a good chance are related to the one described above so I'm adding them here instead of creating new issues:

1. As above, set up a board with a date property which is displayed on cards.
2. Create a new card, set the date property when creating it, and close it.
3. Observe that the value of the date property isn't displayed on the card.
4. If you disable and re-enable displaying the date property on the board, the value shows up.
5. Moving on, drag and drop the card to a different column.
6. Observe that the value of the date property is _erased_. It's not just not displayed on the board; if you open the card you will see that it is _gone_.

Regarding the last observation above, in previous versions I've seen a date property revert to a previous value when a card is moved to a new column, so that's also something to watch out for when fixing these issues.
title: Bug: Changed date property not reflected in board card in macOS app

body:
Hi,

I have been using Focalboard for a year now. I usually login in every few months to update some things. Watchtower is automatically updating the Docker container of Focalboard. However, today I encountered an unexpected issue - I am unable to log in anymore. 

To resolve this, I followed the steps outlined in the "reset password" section of the Focalboard guide (https://www.focalboard.com/guide/admin/). Since the Docker container doesn't have a text editor, I had to install nano and curl. Curl is required for the reset script.

After installing nano and curl, I executed the following script:

bash reset-password.sh name temporarypassword
(Note: I replaced the actual name with 'name' here)

The output I received was:
{"error":"internal server error","errorCode":500} ...

The container's output while trying to reset the password states:

"user not found (name)" "API ERROR." Why has my user disappeared? Is it possible to check which users exist in the db?

body:
## Summary

Within a playbook run, when a tag is used in any of :
1. Checklist title
2. Task title
3. Task description

Users will be able to click the tag to see tagged messages in right sidebar

## How important this is to me and why
One of the shortcomings of playbook runs is the inability to trace (both ways) between checklists/tasks and related messages in an associated channel. Enabling tags would be a "low-hanging-fruit" way to allow playbook run participants to jump to messages related to a checklist/task with one click to get up-to-speed on the related discussion.

Why aren't threads enough? 
1. Threads are easily broken if playbook run participants fail to use Reply even once. And reconnecting messages to a thread is difficult. Tags would make it much easier to bring messages under a checklist/task for easy reference.
2. Connecting a thread to a task can occur through a message link in the task description, but it requires creation of a message for the head of the thread that contains some textual reference to the checklist/task for context (e.g. I've used numbering schemes on checklists+tasks to provide reference IDs). Getting to messages associated with the checklist/task is a two-step process: click on thread head message link and then click on message or replies icon to see thread. Using tags allows users to get to messages related to checklist/task in one click, and would not require creation of a thread head message, so checklist/task tags could be set in the playbook itself, or at least before any messages related to the checklist/task are created.

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Using playbook runs to structure discussions and provide two-way links between messages and related checklists/tasks.
2. 
6.

## Additional context/similar features
* Boeing is using self-hosted Team(?) version 7.5.2, I believe
* I would prefer Mattermost add a more sophisticated way to connect messages to playbook checklists/tasks. E.g. native checklist/task linking support (similar to channels, messages, boards, cards). From a checklist/task, I'd like to be able to "See checklist/task thread", "Reply to task" to add to task-related thread. From a message, I'd like to be about to "Mark outcome/decision" as a way to flag the message as the "outcome/decision" related to a task.

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
title: Feature Idea: make tags work in playbook checklists

body:
## Overview

I'm running Focalboard in Kubernetes. My database is a separate Postgres 13 VM that uses a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate (setup like [this](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/13/ssl-tcp.html#SSL-SERVER-FILES)).

I was stuck on Focalboard `7.5.2` for a while because of [this](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/4651) bug.  I just tried to upgrade to `7.8.4` and was met with the error below. This occurs on `7.9.3` also.



```
2023/04/07 18:09:59 readConfigFile
2023/04/07 18:09:59 {ServerRoot:http://localhost:8000 Port:8000 DBType:postgres DBConfigString:postgres://username:password@db05.internal.mydomain.com:5432/dbfocalboard?sslmode=verify-full&connect_timeout=10 DBTablePrefix: UseSSL:false SecureCookie:false WebPath:./pack FilesDriver:local FilesS3Config:{AccessKeyID: SecretAccessKey: Bucket: PathPrefix: Region: Endpoint: SSL:false SignV2:false SSE:false Trace:false Timeout:0} FilesPath:/data/files MaxFileSize:0 Telemetry:true TelemetryID: PrometheusAddress: WebhookUpdate:[] Secret: SessionExpireTime:2592000 SessionRefreshTime:18000 LocalOnly:false EnableLocalMode:true LocalModeSocketLocation:/var/tmp/focalboard_local.socket EnablePublicSharedBoards:false FeatureFlags:map[] EnableDataRetention:false DataRetentionDays:0 TeammateNameDisplay:username ShowEmailAddress:false ShowFullName:false AuthMode:native LoggingCfgFile: LoggingCfgJSON: AuditCfgFile: AuditCfgJSON: NotifyFreqCardSeconds:0 NotifyFreqBoardSeconds:0}
info  [2023-04-07 18:09:59.677 Z] Focalboard server                        caller="model/version.go:60" version=7.8.4 edition=linux build_number=dev build_date=n/a build_hash=6fc1d9f8db9d366a16cd0ce18d34a5d7b8d5b1e5
error [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] Database Ping failed                     caller="server/server.go:220" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
fatal [2023-04-07 18:09:59.684 Z] server.NewStore ERROR                    caller="main/main.go:136" error="x509: certificate signed by unknown authority"
  main.main
      /go/src/focalboard/server/main/main.go:136
  runtime.main
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/proc.go:250
  runtime.goexit
      /usr/local/go/src/runtime/asm_amd64.s:1571

```

I had to change `sslmode=verify-full` to `sslmode=require` to get Focalboard to start.  From [this](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/lib/pq#hdr-Connection_String_Parameters) page for the Go Postgres driver:

> Valid values for sslmode are:
>  *  disable - No SSL
>  *  require - Always SSL (skip verification)
>  *  verify-ca - Always SSL (verify that the certificate presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA)
>  *  verify-full - Always SSL (verify that the certification presented by the server was signed by a trusted CA and the server host name matches the one in the certificate)

It is now working with `7.8.4` and `7.9.3`. Did something change with the included package of `ca-certificates` in the Docker image? The SSL cert I'm using is signed by Let's Encrypt, so it should be accepted everywhere...


## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Run Postgres in a separate VM using a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate
2. Try to run Focalboard `7.8.4` in Docker using `sslmode=verify-full`  in the Postgres connection string
3. Error

## Expected behavior

Focalboard should accept CA certificates from Let's Encrypt.

## Screenshots (optional)

N/A

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.8.4
 - Browser and OS: Firefox+Chrome on Linux (Kubernetes)

## Additional context (optional)

N/A

body:
## Summary

I'm just starting using Mattermost Board (Focalboard in Matterrmost) in one of the companies. I'd like to keep all my workflow with this program, but I see a potential issue for the future:
My workflow is not a single project, but contiguous. I won't close it soon, but I plan to work with it for years. So after some time I got dozens of closed tasks (visible in `Done` column), and after a year there will be hundreds.
I'd like to have a function to archive `Done` tasks, not to include them in current view, but have them in history.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

## Additional context/similar features

So far, a kind of "override" is to create a column (e.g. `Archived`), and hide it.

In other programs, the function is implemented:
- Jira: "close" issue
- Wekan, Kanboard: archive task / card
title: Feature Idea: Possibility to archive done tasks

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Set the theme to dark theme (not an issue in light/default theme)
2. Open a board where cards use the `Assignee` field
3. Select/create a card and note the text in the `Assignee` field matches the background color

## Expected behavior

In the dark theme, most text should inherit its color using the CSS variable `--center-channel-color-rgb: 220, 220, 220;`. However, this does not seem to be the case for the Assignee field. In my testing, the text in this field is visible in lighter themes. It appears as if the RGB value of `51,51,51` is hardcoded in the styling for that item rather than using the theme variable.

I tried hard reloading with a clean cache, new browsers, and incognito windows, and the issue persisted.

## Screenshots (optional)

### Original
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26947869/223825004-669636ab-bcb5-458e-809a-edbc82937035.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26947869/223825080-1a500371-6884-4228-890e-b4144fd2363a.png)

### Light Theme

## Summary

2. The board will be persistent, and the items can be archived, so they are no longer obstructing view, but they will still be there, in case we want to check some discussion, or content below them.
3. `Trust the user, but assume that user wants to harm you` approach.

## Additional context/similar features

Items in Jira cannot be removed, but can be hidden, and found in the future.

body:
## Summary

I'm implementing Focalboard across my company, and we created our own templates. We don't plan on ever using the stock/example templates that come with it, so it's quite an inconvenient the fact that all our templates are positioned further down the list and we have to scroll every time we create a new board.

If we were able to either delete/hide standard templates or just rearrange them to be listed further down when compared to our custom ones, UX would be greatly improved.

Furthermore, most of our custom templates strongly inherit from the standard ones, so having the option to edit them as well wouldn't be bad at all.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance:Medium

This is, objectively, a UX improvement that can save an organization considerable time depending on how large their team is.

Use cases:
1. User wants a cleaner template selection UI.
2. User will never use Focalboard's standard templates.
3. User wants to customize standard templates to their own cases/realities.

## Additional context/similar features

Looking very briefly at the code, this functionality [Editing/Deleting] would be very similar to the one already present on custom boards. Reordering, on the other hand, would be a different implementation, but definitely not something complex.
title: Feature Idea: Edit/Reorder/Delete Stock Templates

body:
## Summary

The current UI on the Boards share dialog confuses users and is inconsistent with the behavior of adding explicit members to the board. The team role behaves as an option to "allow team members to join the board", but the UI can make it seem like you are adding the entire team as explicit members of the board, which is not the case. However, when adding individual board members, you are actually adding them as explicit members of the board.

We should consider improving the team role section to make it clearer that it is an option to **open the board to the team**, but not adding the team to the board.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Users granting their entire team to the board
2. Users adding their entire team to the board

## Additional context/similar features

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/212103221-d73b54e2-50bd-4a55-b748-af49df07efc7.png)
title: Feature Idea: Share boards team access UI is misleading

body:
Suggested by QA community contributor @jprusch during Boards v7.7 feature testing

## Summary

New boards are always created under the "Boards" category but this is not always the preferred behavior.  If I have created a new Category and then want to create a board under that category, I'd expect it to be added to that category. I'd like this behavior changed as it makes sense to create the board under the category I'm working in.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium.  There is the "Move to" function where Boards can be moved to another category but this is just an extra step that wastes time and shouldn't be required.

Use cases:
1. Creating boards within the Category I'm working in
