body:
## Summary

Add the ability to zoom in on images that are in the card description.

- I am currently viewing a card description that contains images
- I hover over an image with my mouse
- My mouse cursor changes appearance to indicate that the image is clickable
- I click on the image and it opens either:
  - in a popup
  - in a new tab

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

I often add lots of images to my cards when using focalboard as a project management tool instead of using it as a kanban. It is very convenient as a knowledge base and I often add big images that have small text, which therefore can't be read from inside a card popup.

Use cases:
1. Being able to read small text on high dimension images
2. Being able to download an image that has been added into a card description

## Additional context/similar features

Currently, the only way to download/zoom a card description image is to use the user's browser's devtools.

Example :
Click the first image that appears on this page : https://lokeshdhakar.com/projects/lightbox2/
title: Feature Idea: zooming-in images in card description

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Login to Boards as User A and open a board (i.e., `Board 123`)
2. Go the the Share dialog and add User B to `Board 123` as any role
3. In a separate incognito browser, login as User B and go to `Board 123`
4. Go back to the window with User A and remove User B from the board
5. Go to window with User B and see error - user is taken to the template picker
6. As User A, add User B back to `Board 123`
7. Go to window with User B again and see error - `Board 123` is automatically opened for User B after User A added them back to the board
8. Repeat steps 1-7 on the template editor and see the same issue there as well.

## Expected behavior
**Step 5:** When a user is removed from a board, and they have other boards in their sidebar, they should be taken to the next available board on the sidebar.
**Step 7:** The board should be added back to User B's sidebar, but NOT automatically open for them. User B should remain on their current view.

## Edition and Platform
 - Edition: Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.7.0
 - Browser and OS: Chrome on Mac

body:
## Summary
Sync with Notion.  I really love focalboard, and it's simplicity.  I also love notion and it's note-taking features and database functionality.  They recently introduced database sync for Jira (laughable choice I know) and Github Issues & PR.  It would be amazing if focalboard could sync with Notion's database.   Here's a github example https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync.
In my opinion there are no good all in one project mangament platforms (chat, wiki/notes/witeboard, kanban), at best the platforms out there are able to combine two of those (like MM & focalboard), but people are left trying to stitch things together (and is a reason why slack is still so populare despitet zero improvements in years).  While MM is the best chat plattform IMO, focalboard isn't as fully featured as others, and the platform as a whole lacks any good capability/integrations with note-taking/md/wiki tools.
As Notion only has those two integrations for db sync right now, I think it would help spread awareness to users about this project and help people see the light (most people I've spoke to love MM, but are unaware about focalboard).

What the new capability is.
Sync Notion database with focalboard

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High
I really want to use focalboard, as I love Mattermost and the project as a whole- but it just doesn't have enough features & integratitons compared to the alternatives.  This would change that, focalboard would be tigtly integrated with our chat, and make it easier to keep projects updated.

Use cases:
1. Add extra info/attach documents in Notion
2. Keep projects synced, and your team far more organized
3. Allow (some) users to stay inside mattermost while still staying on the same page

## Additional context/similar features

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
You can try it at notion.so with github, but here's the code: https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js/tree/main/examples/notion-github-sync

My major concern is how conflicts happen, both on focalboard's end and notion, considering they integrated it with jira I'm tempted to believe it's possible though.
title: Feature Idea: Sync Boards with Notion

Similar to #3096, check `make swagger` in CI to verify the API docs stay up to date. The embedded Swagger YAML definitions inside Golang comments can be a bit fragile with vague error messages being hard to follow.

Rough CI steps:

1. `make swgger` (this may error on it's own)
2. Check if changes e.g. `git status --porcelain` or `git diff --exit-code`

body:
Guys, I have a few machines running Windows 7 SP1 x64, with Net 4.8 and WebView2, yet Focalboard refuses to launch. Yes, that OS is out of Silicon Valley favour, Microsoft hegemon urges to spend time and money for software and hardware upgrades I do not need, but frankly could not care less because _it works_.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/373718/200879782-32d38cae-23f6-4558-b822-98d9f0dc2d3a.png)

So what sauce do you have under the hood that requires Windows 10 specifically? Or it is just a matter of compilation flag? If it's about the latter, then please consider adding that flag to make more people productive beyond the so-called golden billion.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading any board causes a runtime exception as follows:

```
TypeError: Intl.Locale is not a constructor
```

## Root cause

This is a vanilla web compatibility bug with older versions of Firefox due to missing API implementations. (I am operating Focalboard under a proprietary, restricted environment where a browser upgrade is not feasible.)

## Proposal

There exists a polyfill for this API: https://formatjs.io/docs/polyfills/intl-locale/

I fixed this issue locally with the following patch:

```diff
diff --git a/webapp/package.json b/webapp/package.json
index 25843393..832b49d7 100644
--- a/webapp/package.json
+++ b/webapp/package.json
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@
                "@draft-js-plugins/editor": "^4.1.2",
                "@draft-js-plugins/emoji": "^4.6.0",
                "@draft-js-plugins/mention": "^5.1.2",
+               "@formatjs/intl-locale": "^3.0.7",
                "@fullcalendar/core": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/daygrid": "^5.10.1",
                "@fullcalendar/interaction": "^5.10.1",
diff --git a/webapp/src/main.tsx b/webapp/src/main.tsx
index dd9a3168..e4a9ee32 100644
--- a/webapp/src/main.tsx
+++ b/webapp/src/main.tsx
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 // Copyright (c) 2015-present Mattermost, Inc. All Rights Reserved.
 // See LICENSE.txt for license information.
+import '@formatjs/intl-locale/polyfill'
 import React from 'react'
 import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'
 import {Provider as ReduxProvider} from 'react-redux'
```

I'd like to propose something like this: it offers improved webcompat with minimal additional code, and with minor bundle size cost.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to the root path of the web server, i.e. localhost:8000
2. See a blank page and an error.

## Expected behavior

I see the welcome screen.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Server
 - Version: 7.4.3
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 68, Ubuntu 20.04

## Symptom

Loading the main page causes a syntax error while parsing the main.js bundle due to an illegal regular expression.

```
SyntaxError: invalid identity escape in regular expression
```

## Root cause

This is due to an illegal regular expression escape used here:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/v7.4.3/webapp/src/blocks/boardView.ts#L62

## Proposal

A code search suggests that `sortBoardViewsAlphabetically` is not meaningfully used in the codebase; it only has callsites in unit tests.

It seems that it was previously used in `webapp/src/components/sidebar/sidebarBoardItem.tsx`, however as of aa540e73ce669a1acd491a5edd7d55199ef1b87c, its use was decommissioned and the original utility was not removed.

Removing this function altogether remediates the SyntaxError issue in my local environment. I propose this method and associated unit tests simply be removed.

### People Data Types should auto filter

I'm setting up a new board and finding that the `person` attribute doesn't auto filter based on who's a member of the card. Any thoughts / plans to have this auto filter to the people in the board or last used few first, then let it search?

_Issue created from a [Mattermost message](https://community.mattermost.com/_redirect/pl/1sy7ax7ye3fc9ymyh5pbo5xt8h) *by @coltoneshaw*._
title: Enhancement: List Board Members before Non-Board Members

body:
## Summary
Board templates are only supported in English currently. If users switch their language settings to a non-English language, the standard templates will always appear in English regardless of the language selected. Board users rely on standard templates to help them learn about the product and to get started quickly. 

For non-native English speakers looking to use Boards, this is problematic because it prevents them from learning and adopting the tool in the language they are most comfortable with. The mixed languages also creates a poor and inconsistent experience while using Boards.

#### What the new capability is:
When a non-English language is selected in the settings menu, display the translated standard templates instead of the English templates. All content on the standard templates should be translated, including:

- Board/template name
- Board description
- View names
- All sample card content


## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Non-native English speakers looking to learn more about Boards using the Welcome template
2. Non-native English speakers creating new boards from any of the standard templates

## Additional context/similar features

See [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/1bhz6ufmu7be5kkxgqp7kbjzsw) for reference.
title: Feature Idea: Translated standard Boards templates

body: ## Summary It's not immediately obvious in the Settings > Import submenu that only the `Import archive` option actually opens the file dialog for you to upload a `.boardarchive` file. The other menu options are simply links to specific sections in the docs. I'd like to make it more clear in the UI the purpose that the menu options serve. Perhaps an icon that would help infer to users that a menu option is a _link_? cc @wuwinson @asaadmahmood

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to focalboard
2. Open a card
3. Type a comment
4. Delete a single character with backspace
5. Press ctrl+Z

## Expected behavior

I expect ctrl+Z to restore the character i deleted. Instead, nothing happens.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: the one on the community server, as of today
 - Version: v7.4.2
 - Browser and OS: both on Firefox 105.0.3, and on the Mattermost desktop client. OS is Ubuntu 22.04

## Additional context

This is Sev 3 (minor, non-critical functionality), but it can become a bit frustrating when typing long comments in particular. E.g. something gets deleted by mistake, and it cannot be restored in any way. I am currently typing my comments on a text editor first, then copypasting them into the card, in order to avoid running into this issue again.

A few cases in which ctrl+Z didn't work:
- Type a comment -> delete single character -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted character, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+backspace to delete the last word -> press ctrl+Z (it should restore the deleted word, but doesn't)
- Type a comment -> press ctrl+Z (should delete the last word or letter that was typed, but doesn't)

Interestingly, there's also a case in which ctrl+Z worked well:
- Type a comment -> select it all with ctrl+A -> press backspace to delete it -> press ctrl+Z (it successfully restores the full deleted comment, as expected)
title: Bug: ctrl+Z doesn't always undo

## Summary

Currently, when moving a board to another category from the sidebar, I can only select existing categories. There may be cases where I want to move the board to a new category. To do so, I'd first have to create the new category, then go back to the Options Menu (...) from the sidebar and move the board.

If we can add a "Create New Category" option under the existing categories in the menu (similar to Channels), it would allow users to easily move boards to a newly created category.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:

body: Broken out of #3812. We have some error handling/routing logic that does variations of `/boards/error` and `/plugins/focalboard/error` that under the right circumstances will do a infinite recursive redirect loop (see video in original issue). To do: - determine condition(s) that cause this breakage - fix logic to prevent unnecessary redirects A thought: might be worth holding off until multi-product architecture ships as we'll no longer be a plugin in the event that changes some of the URLs or client side errors.
title: Investigate `/error` handling logic to prevent recursive redirects

body:
## Summary

The [Person Property](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/webapp/src/properties/person/person.tsx) and [MultiPerson Property](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/webapp/src/properties/multiperson/multiperson.tsx ) share most of the same code. 

Especially after this [PR](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/pull/3888) made them work in an equivalent manner. We should implement a "base Person property" and update Person and MultiPerson to call this property with its specific needs. 

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

As a sameple, see how the various text properties (Text, Email, Phone, and Number) use BaseTextEditor.
title: Code Debt: Share common code between Person and MultiPerson properties

See [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/dt6kshfaj3yc5j5ceit4x4naur) for details.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create or go to a board in table view
2. Ensure that the view has an editable text column
3. Attempt to drag to select text from within that column

## Expected behaviour

The text is selected. Instead, the whole row is picked up and dragged, and if you drop it, it often gets moved even if you drop it in the same place you started.

At a higher level, I'd expect clicking and dragging the mouse to only trigger text selection as long as the cursor is within an editable textbox. I'd only expect the whole row to get dragged when first clicking outside of an editable field.

## Edition and Platform

- Boards plugin on community in MM Desktop App on Mac
- Focalboard 0.15.2 (33) standalone on Mac

## Additional context

body:
Hello!

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Team Edition + Mattermost Boards (plugin)
 - Version: 7.1.1 + 7.1.0

## Description

We are considering Mattermost with Mattermost Boards Plugin as a solution in our company. The main concern is the email notifications for any actions in cards and due dates. Right now all the communication between Boards and Mattermost (and end user) are based on the messages passed through Boards bot.

If you check all the messages in the direct message history it looks like a flow of separate notifications. It doesn't look like threads or something to group or separate differend boards and cards messages from the complete bot conversation feed.

Also you will nevere get message in email inbox until "you’ve been offline for more than 60 seconds or away from Mattermost for more than five minutes". You cannot control this setting.

The special case is due dates - there is no notifications at all.

It is very important for us while you working on several separate projects at the same time and get a lot of notifications be able to track your tasks events without distracting from your work process. When you get all the info into your GMail inbox it much more easier (also you can search in your inbox to track taks history, for example).

What we expect and why it is important:
1. Be able to control email notifications in Mattermost and especially Mattermost Boards plugin and be able to set it "always" (without rules of away/offline for a certain amount of time). It is important that the end users will be notified guaranteed and know that every notification can be found in their inbox.
2. Receive notification of the due date. Also it will be good to customize notifications i.e. get notification in 1 day or somethig before due date will arrive. Also it can be applied to start date (in case of start date->end date time interval set).

## Additional questions
1. Do you have that functionality in your roadmap?
2. Also how it can be customized on the our end - for example custom bot or something?
title: Feature Idea: email notifications

body:
## Summary

Currently I am working on make a custom theme for focalboard, but find that the the theme is changed by color variables in inline styles, this is not friendly to secondary development.

light mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-40](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049574-5e616e5d-73ec-4f4d-bfb6-1ab70d6d5257.png)

when change to night mode

![截图 2022-08-05 17-34-58](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34258753/183049604-7327438d-6d88-4690-bbf9-61a531b190de.png)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Convenient for third-party theme development

## Additional context/similar features

I checked the related issue and learned that focalboard does not plan to support the import of custom css files in the short term, so I decided to fork this project, and then make some changes I want to by this "hack" way, but now I find that focalboard does not support custom css files. There is so little support that even css variables are provided inline, which frustrates me.

It's not required, and focalboard will always be an excellent project even without support from 3rd party themes. But it's a good idea for the team to let the project team focus on developing the core functionality and then open up permissions for other interested people to develop the theme, which also benefit more users.

So I hope you can think more carefully about this issue.
title: Feature Idea: Add the switching method of class-level night mode and day mode

body:
## Summary

It's not immediately clear in the linking modal/process how linking works, in terms of what it is, and why to do it. Most channels will already have linked boards so the default "landing page" won't be displayed. 

It also seems a bit too easy to link a board to a new channel. I wanted to test it but it seemed like it could really break things for the currently linked channel. For example - how would they know the board that they had linked had been removed and re-linked elsewhere? If they try to link it back, the channel that it's now linked to would lose it without notification. Sort of like a boards tug of war. 

## How important this is to me and why

High - this may impact user experience and cause frustration.

## Additional context/similar features

I propose adding help text to the "Link board" modal. Something along the lines of:

"Linking a board adds it to the channel's RHS for easy access. Read our docs for more information." and the docs can provide a detailed breakdown of how it works and the use cases.
title: Feature Idea: UX improvement for linking channels

body:
## Summary

When pasting/adding images into a card, there's no way to preview that image or even download it. Due to this nature, it can be hard to review attached images or even download them to preview them in another application.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. When an image is attached to a card as content, when clicking on the image, it should open up another modal with the image preview
2. From there, the user could then be able to view the image in full size or "fitted" to their screen size and allow them to zoom
3. In the preview they could even download the image for a number of reasons

1. When clicking on the three dots beside an image, it would be nice to have a download option - or even implement one that displays when hovering over images to save a few clicks

## Additional context/similar features

A project I'm currently working on requires sharing of mockup images between designers and developers. Unfortunately, while we can attach images, there's no way for the developers to "enhance" the image in the card to view the full design. This has left us with having to share images outside of the card and including a link to that external source. It would be nice to have it as an all-in-one system to remove the need for an external dependency. Other than that, it's been great!!
title: Feature Idea: Image preview/download for attached images

body:
## Summary

Looks like Focalboard doesn't utilize OpenGraph so when links are shared it's a missed opportunity to tell people about the products.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Low

I just shared Focalboard at work since I thought it looked very promising, but the reality is most people probably aren't going to click on a random link just because I shared it. I'd expect the embedded data from OpenGraph to pitch the product/website and entice people to click it.

Unfortunately, in this case it didn't do that. ^-^'

Use cases:
1. Gives Focalboard/Mattermost more opportunity to explain what it is, especially without the user having to click a link first.

## Additional context/similar features

Most websites. ^-^'

This just looks a bit sad. 🥲 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613027-49d2d895-e80e-489d-b793-520ed8c02e85.png)

On the other hand, here are examples from other projects I shared in a group chat.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22801583/180613149-c629048e-f5da-49b6-b3ea-1a260f2da075.png)

body:
## Summary

It would be really cool to translate any new or edited issue into focalboard cards. In this way one might have a single board that holds both, the code issues from gitlab, and the tasks discussed and distributed across members from the related Mattermost Channel.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. A project requires the development of code (e.g., a data analysis in R). This code is hosted on GitLab and an MM channel is linked to that project. Code related issues are generated directly at GitLab or in the IDE that is used for code development. However, the discussion of additional tasks that are not directly linked to the code (e.g., brainstormings, tasks to retrieve additional data) takes place at MM. A focal board at MM that automatically shows the GitLab issues as well as the MM tasks as cards would be great to have the big picture at hand.
2. The same can happen for Manuscript writing. The LaTeX sources are hosted at GitLab, writing issues are distributed as issues among authors, while discussions on what to include in the text, which journal to select, will take place at MM and may also end up in tasks. Having all GitLab issues and assigned MM tasks in one board would make the whole writing process really transparent.

## Additional context/similar features

I did not came across some so far.
title: Translate GitLab issues into FocalBoard cards

body:
## Summary

Ability to hide certain properties on a per-card level, so users can hide properties not relevant to the specific card. Properties are additive on a board, and if certain properties are needed for a select few cards, they get applied to all cards on the board, but users may not want to see all the properties on every card. Deleting a property on one card also deletes it for all cards.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Multi-purpose board where many properties are available, but not all of them are relevant or needed for every card on the board.

## Additional context/similar features
See this [thread](https://community.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/7hzunk4kuprhpq6cappejnje7o) from @michaelgamble for more details:

> Also completely different topic, I've been thinking about my use of boards / cards - one feature I'm thinking i would like is the ability to define different card types that basically define different sets of properties for each card type... 
> 
> The reason I'm thinking about this is I have a board right now where I'm tracking:
> 
> - Tasks
> - Stories
> - Bugs
> - Component Releases
> - Text Font Releases
> - Icon Releases
> 
> Each of these use cases have a slightly different set of requirements in terms of data fields as properties for example "Icon Releases" would have a URL for GitHub PR - but many of the other ones don't....  IF i want to see them in the same board, I'm forced to keep adding more and more and more properties to a card in order to manage the different types and its very quickly feeling like it could grow out of hand... 
> 
> I'm not sure what the solution is but in quickly thinking about it, makes me think the solution might lay in the "having a card on multiple boards" explorations you guys are approaching.. the only problem though is i'm pretty sure the requirement you've currently set out is that they all need to have all properties additively matching...
> 
> The reason I'm bringing this up here is maybe there is a case for not adding all the property fields, but some other in-between solution maybe (have not put a lot of thought into this)... perhaps identifying which fields will be common on the board that has cards from multiple sources??
> 
> Anyways food for though.. just wanted to share... oh and also i was thinking of the parallel of jira and issue types while visualizing this
title: Feature Idea: Hide properties on cards/card templates

## Summary

Support for 
Calendar Feed or CalDAV 

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

I think the feature to have a calendar feed, to be able to sync the calendar of focal board to third party calendars is fundamental.  This way the cards with a date on it would appear in the calendar of your devices automatically

Use cases:
1. Integrate with system calendar, along side your other calendars in one single view
2. Add a card with a date on it and receive notifications in all your devices automatically


Any examples of good implementations of this capability
Trello does this with a calendar power up, exports a calendar feed that calendar on the Mac/iOS/ipadOS/WatchOS can add and have it show on all devices

## Summary

A way to limit the maximum number of cards that can be moved to a column in board view, to prevent project overload and help teams focus on completing existing tasks before moving on to new ones.

1. Option to restrict the number of cards in a column
2. Option to restrict the number of cards per assignee or other property values in a column
3. Option to change the color of the column when the limit is reached

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Setting WIP limits on card status, so only a set number of cards can be in "In Progress" or "In Review"
2. Setting WIP limits on assignees, so an assignee cannot have more than X number of cards assigned to them at any one time.

## Additional context/similar features

- https://www.atlassian.com/agile/kanban/wip-limits
- https://djaa.com/patterns-of-kanban-maturity/
title: Feature Idea: WIP Limits for Kanban boards

body:
## Summary
The text property currently doesn't support text wrapping or multi-line text, so longer text becomes difficult to read. Full text is only visible as a tooltip on hover. To improve the text property, we can consider some of the following options:

1. Text wrapping with an auto-expanding field, so users don't have to keep scrolling to the right and the entire text value is always visible on the card
2. Expand/minimize button, so user can see the full text when they want to without impacting the card layout
3. Auto-expanding to a limit, with button to "Show All" - Combo of option 1 and 2

Open to other suggestions as well!

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Inputting long text values
4. Users who use the `Text` property as a note field

## Additional context/similar features
![Screen Shot 2022-05-26 at 5 41 43 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/170584795-509b16c1-2ada-45aa-bdb0-fb8cb5555502.png)
title: Feature Idea: Text property auto wrapping/expanding

body:
## Summary

I found we've been using other tools for these retrospective / quadrant view, and realized that this would be a perfect use case for boards.. if each quadrant could be like the current kanban, where you can add items, order them, and drag and drop them into quadrants.. board cards would be perfect.

The things that makes boards more powerful then other tools in my opinion is four fold:

1. being able to have a continued conversation on a card
2. being able to create multiple views for taking action on items as outcomes of the retrospective
3. being able to have integration for the sake of cross posting
4. the power of being able to replicate the card into other boards (coming in that future feature)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High/Medium/Low

Use cases:
1. Retrospective quadrants
2. market fit quadrants
3. magic quadrants
4. the list goes on and on...

## Additional context/similar features

Right now we use figjam for retrospectives, but the usefulness of it ends because the follow up needs to happen outside of that ecosystem.. in our case inside the MM ecosystem.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31866506/170327533-13408569-ac22-4590-9cf2-4dcbf8528bcd.png)
title: Feature Idea: Retrospective / Quadrant View

body:
## Summary

Firstly thank you all for building such an amazing tool.
Our team is now using focalboard as our daily agile kanban. We show this in a pretty big TV and do daily scrum in front of it. However its header and side panel do affect its useful viewport. Only ~70% of the screen is being used.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. See summary.

## Additional context/similar features
We're currently inject a script at our nginx side
```
sub_filter '</body>' '</body><script>$script</script>';
sub_filter_once on;
```
The script is actually a simple JS
```
document.getElementsByClassName("top-head")[0].hidden = true; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Sidebar octo-sidebar")[0].style.display = "none"; 
document.getElementsByClassName("Kanban")[0].style.marginLeft = 0;
```
We understand it's really a hack. Just for your reference.
title: Feature Idea: Show a kanban in fullscreen mode

body:
While completing #2900 I found the root cause of #2898 to be that `Utils.buildURL` was modified and doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installs even if the 2nd arg `absolute` is present and `true`. At a glance, the pattern inside the codebase seems to be to use `buildURL` to build a relative URL and append it to a URL with a base instead of building an absolute URL string all in one go.

e.g.

```ts
const url = new URL(window.location.origin)
url.pathname = Utils.buildURL('/foo?bar=baz', true)
```

I tracked the regression in `buildURL` to this diff:

https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/pull/2406/files#diff-9e0f028747bfe61f41e35d96170706c27451268275f8dd0303a77986ae329916R520-R523 (`blame` source: https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blame/9001c3576f1cd13da6953b5c193e0cd7bd712de6/webapp/src/utils.ts#L520)

The intention of this ticket is to gauge whether some sort of refactor to `buildURL` and it's usages is worth it and then the scope of how to proceed with that.
title: Code Debt: `Utils.buildURL` doesn't build absolute URLs in non-plugin installations

body:
The `latest` Docker tag is a side-effect of the latest _build_ from `main`, not the latest _release_. We're looking to update it so `latest` is triggered off of _releases_ so it's reasonably more stable. This stemmed from #2592.

The pattern is to have building/tagging/releasing defined in GitLab CI so we'll need to migrate from our config in hub.docker.com. Relevant community thread: https://community-daily.mattermost.com/private-core/pl/bfx4n8dpu3gymybsdm545mpurw
title: Bug: Normalize & migrate Docker tags to GitLab CI

body:
Big thanks for your awesome work in advance!

## Summary

Currently there is no overwrite protection when two people edit one block at the same time: if you defocus a block it gets auto-saved and your local state overwrites potential edits by others. As I think true collaboration (editing at the same time, seeing changes live, ...) is very difficult to implement and comes with other downsides (stable internet connection, performance, ...?) **I would suggest to just lock a block, when someone else has the same block in focus.** 

## How important this is to me and why

_Importance: High_
To me and my team this issue is of extraordinary importance. It is not only the loss of data but also the trust in the tool that makes this a problem for us. See the example:

Use cases:
1. Person A has a block opened and in focus
2. Person B typed loads of content in the same block (like a transcript)
3. A now closes the block to save the content
4. B does not get the update as the block is still open, closes the block and therefore overwrites all edits by A 


## How it could be solved

So I imagine (from a user's perspective, I don't know about the technical details) a lock icon appearing on the left of the block, when someone is already editing this block, hindering me to enter the block at all. 


I use focalsboards as part of a mattermost install on yunohost (v 6.5.0)
Thanks a lot and keep up the good work!
title: Feature Idea: Locking blocks for better collaboration

body:
## Summary

- Allow setting zoom level as we can do currently with CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons.

- I think adding zoom level adjustment will increase the productivity and visibility of boards. 

- As it occurred to me when we have more columns in board we have to scroll on the right side to see the entire board but instead if zoom level is present then it will improve visibility of entire board properly. 

- My suggestion is if this feature is going to be implemented then it should be placed around top right corner near feedback placeholder.

- Zoom level should be from 10% - 100% [or maximum zoom can be more than 100%].

- It should also retain zoom level after application is closed.

- Currently we can do this adjustment via CTRL + Mouse Scroll buttons, but unfortunately this gets reset on restart.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:

1. When more columns are present in board. In my case I have (5 columns) very low, low, medium, high, super high priorities in tasks and 8 columns in task status [backlog, in development, analysis, in progress, testing, finished, not finished, parked]. Viewing by priority or status requires horizontal scrolling.

2. When working on generally popular screen resolution of 1366 X 768 with above conditions.

3. When we need entire board to be visible without horizontally scrolling for more fields.

Above mentioned some of the use cases can be fulfilled by adding zoom level adjustment feature.
title: Feature Idea: Adding Application Zoom Level Adjustment

## Summary

Pretty much inspired by #1756 ("_Auto-sync card with GitHub_"):

> Created from [this thread](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/d99j4u4eu3dkueg1ta5qrn9jxe). It would be great to have a card be automatically created when an issue is created on a Github repo; and conversely, a Github issue be created when we create a card in MM.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

## Additional context/similar features

- It should allow to use self hosted instances
- [GitLab's Issues API is well documented](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/api/issues.html)
- [Developers can reference issues in their commits](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/issues/managing_issues.html#closing-issues-automatically) and if a corresponding GitLab issue would be closed, the twin issue in Focalboard could be automatically closed as well (this would also be a tiny workaround for #624 since issues might have a direct GitLab reference)

body:
## Summary

In a chroot webhosting environment it is not reliably possible to listen on an ip, because other users could use the same port. This can be easily solved with a unix socket

Which is the new capability.

focalboard-server optionally listens on a unix socket

## How important is it for me and why?

Importance: Medium

Any examples of good implementations of this capability.
```
{
    "serverRoot": "unix:///tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard.sock",
    "port": none,
    "dbtype": "sqlite3",
    "dbconfig": "./focalboard.db",
    "postgres_dbconfig": "dbname=focalboard sslmode=disable",
    "useSSL": false,
    "webpath": "./pack",
    "filespath": "./files",
    "telemetry": false,
    "prometheus_address": ":9092",
    "session_expire_time": 2592000,
    "session_refresh_time": 18000,
    "localOnly": false,
    "enableLocalMode": false,
    "localModeSocketLocation":"/tmp/.service/html%2Ffocalboard-admin.sock"
}
```
title: Feature Idea: launch on unix socket

body:
Currently is seems users can only subscribe to individual cards. This is nice, but not at all a viable solution for large boards and/or large teams where people need or want to be notified about all changes. For example I just had 3 people join a board that has 181 cards. In order to get notifications they would need to click on 543 cards and follow them individually. That's a minimum of 1629 clicks and a lot of scrolling. When they are done they still won't be notified of new cards and will need to find and follow each as they get added. Every time a new team member joins they have to go through the whole thing.

I suggest a mechanism to follow a whole board instead of only following on a per-card basis.

Somewhat related, issue #563 suggests a notification channel per board.
title: Feature Idea: Follow boards instead of just cards

## Summary

Checklist items are currently only one line long, so if the text goes beyond the width of the modal, then the user has to highlight and scroll to see the full text content.

Long text on checkboxes should wrap to the next line if if exceeds the width of the card modal, so users can easily see the content on the checkboxes without needing to highlight and scroll.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Checkboxes are used to create subtasks and checklists, so supporting text wrapping will make the feature more usable.

Use cases:
1. [Long text on checkboxes](https://community.mattermost.com/core/pl/z4fix56jzpfxjk6zd58wa1nato)

body:
## Summary

It would be lovely to have the ability to _import_ redmine issues as focal board cards.
Perhaps based on projects/categories (or a custom field)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

At our organisation, we use mattermost for all our internal comms and Redmine for project management. We have just begun to integrate focalboard (or rather mattermost boards) into our workflows. This integration would vastly expand the use-cases of focal board for us.

Use cases:
1.  For teams looking to move their project management from redmine to focal board
2.  For teams needing a constant flow from different redmine projects to different focalboard/mattermost boards
3. The work on such a plugin will easily lend itself to adaptations to other integrations -- a mattermost command, a teello API script, etc...

## Additional context/similar features

The [mattermost plugins for redmine](https://github.com/altsol/redmine_mattermost) has all the foundations for extracting information from the Redmine API and importing into the Mattermost chat, it can probably re-used and modified to integrate with Boards.
title: Feature Idea: Webhook/Integration with Redmine

body:
## Summary

It's related to dates but not quite to [the calendar view](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/338).

I just stumbled over [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) which is basically a Kanban board but each of the columns is grouping cards by a certain time.
For instance like this:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719045-ac0acc26-eefa-466f-bb2a-6713c727ca2d.png)

Or an overview per week:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/154719734-a270491d-6d93-4aa1-acaa-d5be6f768201.png)

Or see Trello's take on this:

![Calendar_2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177679-8f53145b-461a-423a-9ea9-9cac6e9ecf96.jpg)

![Calendar_3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/372620/165177789-5d9ec1be-ae2b-436b-a73e-0da75e2f78b4.jpg)

In my opinion Focalboard is able to do even more than Timestripe in general but the dynamic grouping gives a different user experience for a Kanban board than the current calendar view.

Maybe this is even possible somehow and I'm just missing the bit how to configure it properly.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: it's just a UX improvement (but obviously so good, that [Timestripe](https://timestripe.com/) made it their business model) and [Trello made it into a popular extension](https://trello.com/power-ups/55a5d917446f517774210011/calendar-power-up).

Use cases:
1. A good time based overview

## Additional context/similar features

The calendar view is somehow similar in regards of having a timely overview
title: Feature Idea: automatic time based grouping views (calendar / date related view)

## Summary

Refer to mattermost-server for a similar deployment.

When I installed the Focalboard personal server, I created a mariadb database instead of using mysql, as I was already using mariadb for nextcloud, and installing mysql along side mariadb would be beyond my know how. I recall having some 502 issue at the time, but I eventually resolved it. 
Focalboard had been working for about a month since then, but is now failing to start after I restarted my debian 10 webserver.

body:
## Summary

The current `Date` property type works great for specifying specific dates or date ranges but it doesn't allow me to capture the more generic concept of duration. I currently am using the `Number` property type with a label that specifies a fixed unit of time but this is not very clean when durations are too large or small and it also does not display the units of time in every view which make it hard to interpret (in the calendar for instance)

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. In order to track how long you expect a given issue to take, it would be useful to have a property type that understood the concept of time, allowed for better interfaces for setting durations and showed units of time in all views

For example - issues with expected work hours of 100 or 0.25 - depending on the view, I might prefer to see `4 day, 4 hours` instead of 100 hours and `15 minutes` instead of 0.25 hours. Similarly - adding some friendly input support would be helpful to auto convert between units (all of these should be accepted and mean the same : 720m, 12h,  0.5d).
title: Feature Idea: Consider adding a Duration Property Type

<details>

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 0db63694..a5e661cf 100644

+++ b/Makefile
@@ -40,6 +40,11 @@ server-linux: ## Build server for Linux.

        cd server; env GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -ldflags '$(LDFLAGS)' -o ../bin/linux/focalboard-server ./main

+       $(eval LDFLAGS += -X "github.com/mattermost/focalboard/server/model.Edition=linux")

index c6b177ab..2d4c3510 100644
--- a/docker/Dockerfile
+++ b/docker/Dockerfile
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 ### Webapp build
-FROM node:16.3.0 as nodebuild
+FROM node:16.3 as nodebuild

 WORKDIR /webapp
 ADD webapp/ /webapp

 ADD . /go/src/focalboard

-RUN  make server-linux
+RUN make server-linux-rpi
 RUN mkdir /data

body:
## Summary

Currently, I can view each single board within each single channel. And within a card, I can have a property to assign a card to somebody.

I would like to see all the cards assigned to me, within 1 single view.

## How important this is to me and why
We use cards to manage todos. This is more efficient to have 1 single view, compared to looking into each single board.

Use cases:
1. We have 2 channels: _Project_ and _Kitchen_
2. In _Project_, 2 cards (TaskA and TaskB) are assigned to me, within the property _Who_
3. In Kitchen, 1 card (TaskC) is assigned to me, within the property _Who_
4. I would love to have a view showing TaskA, TaskB and TaskC together. A simple table would be a great start. And a calendar view would be even better (not a priority though)

## Additional context/similar features
Trello does something similar, as far as I understand: https://help.trello.com/article/819-viewing-all-of-your-cards
title: Feature Idea: View all my cards from all boards

Very prominently indicate that there are more columns available. Include a navigation feature to scroll to these columns which are off screen.


#### Observed behavior (that appears unintentional) 
Boards in last columns, such as those that have a far in the future completion date, are not visible on screen. There is no obvious to me navigation feature to notice them, or to navigate to them. Changing page zoom to 67% "helps".

## Screenshots

Could do but our instance is internal.

## Edition and Platform

 - Edition: Personal Desktop / Personal Server / Mattermost plugin
 - Version: 0.11.0
 - Browser and OS: Firefox 78 on Linux

## Additional context

I believe this can be fixed by adjusting CSS - this is a frontend issue.

Moved from https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server/issues/19303 as suggested by @amyblais

body:
## Summary

The point is not to lose focus on text fields when you press `Caps Lock`. I and probably many more who use several languages configure switching the keyboard layout to the caps lock key for speed and convenience instead of the common `Alt` + `Shift`.

### Expected Behavior:

I print text on a line, switch to another layout by pressing `Caps Lock`, and continue typing text.

### Current behavior:

I am typing text, there is a need to change the layout, I hit on the `Caps Lock` and the cursor loses focus. This results in the text being saved to the database (with a small lag). You have to click on the text field again to continue typing!

Due to the fact that you have to switch the layout very often, this causes very significant inconvenience in work.

The idea of ​​not losing focus when pressing the `Caps Lock` will also be useful in cases where the key is used for its intended purpose — to write characters in uppercase.
title: Enhancement: Don't lose focus on text areas when press Caps Lock

body:
## Summary

As the server gets more sophisticated, we recommend / require multi-user environments to use Postgres instead of SQLite. E.g. there are bugs like https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1841 which are caused by concurrency issues on SQLite. 

As a result, we should default Personal Server installations to Postgres instead of SQLite.
title: Update the dockerfile to deploy Postgres instead of SQLite

body:
MM-T4280 Accessing from the Product Switcher

Steps #1

From Channels click on the product switcher in the top left of the Global Header and click Boards

Expected

Dropdown opens with options to navigate to Channels, Boards and Playbooks

Steps #2

Click on Boards

Expected

User lands on the Dashboard with all the channels the user belongs to, displayed in a grid view

Steps #3

Click on a channel and open a board

Expected

First view is displayed in the centre

Steps #4

Click on the product switcher in the top left of the Global Header and click Channels

Expected

User is taken to Channels

Steps #5

Click on the product switcher in the top left of the Global Header and click Boards

Expected

User is taken to the Boards Dashboard
**Test code arrangement:** ``` it('MM-T4280 Accessing from the Product Switcher', () => { // code }); ``` Note: 1. Use [queries from testing-library](https://testing-library.com/docs/dom-testing-library/api-queries) whenever possible. We share the same philosophy as the [testing-library](https://testing-library.com/) when doing UI automation like "Interact with your app the same way as your users" and so, please follow their guidelines especially when querying an element. If you're interested, please comment here and come [join our "Contributors" community channel](https://community.mattermost.com/core/channels/tickets) on our daily build server, where you can discuss questions with community members and the Mattermost core team. For technical advice or questions, please [join our "Focalboard" community channel](https://community.mattermost.com/core/channels/focalboard). For new contributors, please see our [guide](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard#running-and-testing-the-server).

body:
## Summary

As of v0.10, a user can only add/delete/duplicate views from the dropdown menu within the specific view's page. This menu should be accessible on the sidebar so users can manage their views more easily and intuitively.
![Screen Shot 2021-11-02 at 4 46 19 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/139949275-ea88e16b-5553-45ec-8dc0-b2201fcf859c.png)


## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1.  While toggling through views from the sidebar, a user can quickly delete/duplicate their views without having to click into them.
2. More intuitive and less clicks to create new views directly from the sidebar.

## Additional context/similar features
Board management is currently handled through the sidebar "..." buttons, and may make sense to include similar functionality for Views.
![Screen Shot 2021-11-02 at 4 55 58 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93531870/139950459-02a1d896-6633-43ce-86eb-49d8652b386e.png)
title: Feature Idea: Manage Views from Sidebar

## Summary

When uploading images, they should be processed (resized+compressed+meta data trimmed). This can help reducing loading times, as images are smaller.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Image compression
2. Image Resizing
3. Privacy, when private information is trimmed
4. Loading time improvements

## Additional context/similar features

Most (social media) platforms process images to reduce their size, both resolution and storage size, to provide faster loading times and less storage size. Additionally, thumbnails (smaller images) are sometimes generated to allow quick loading times for gallery-like views.

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior
  
Left pixels of any emoji favicon are cropped on Firefox (using latest version).

## Expected behavior

Like on Chrome, favicon is not cropped.

body:
## Summary

Currently, the `create_at` and `update_at` columns in various tables are nullable on the database level. Ideally, the columns should never have a null value and should reflect the correct creation/update time.

Following changes are needed to support this-
1. Update `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table) to be filled by current timestamp wherever the value is null.
2. Add a not-null constraint on `create_at` and `update_at` columns in all focalboard tables (except blocks history table).

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: Medium
title: Add database level defau;t values for create at and update at columns

## Steps to reproduce the behavior

data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,Name ...
```

or

```txt
Failed to open URI.
The specified location is not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

4. The Snackbar does not get that there was an error and says "Export Complete!"

---

1. Select "Settings"
2. Select  "Export archive"
3. See error in popup window:

```txt
Failed to open URI.
Operation not supported.
data:text/json, ...
```

because the in the README.md described `linux/dist/focalboard-app` path does not exist and running `linux/bin/focalboard-app` starts a completely white window.

body:
## Summary

While adding Unit Tests on [this issue](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/832), I found that we are importing `@testing-library/react` in all the test files to extend the RTL (React Testing Libray) custom jest matchers. `jest` actually provides an easier way to handle this where we can add a setup file that is present for all the tests before they start.

This way, we can add the RTL import there and avoid all tests importing the same.

[Reference](https://jestjs.io/docs/configuration#setupfilesafterenv-array)

## How important this is to me and why

Nothing customer-facing but as a developer, this makes me write tests seamlessly without worrying about those special imports. I would consider this as `High` 

## Additional context/similar features

Not specific ones but I have seen many open source projects using common files for jest RTL imports. 

Would be willing to submit a PR if this sounds good 🙂
title: Feature Idea: add common jest import file to configure RTL imports

body:
## Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Go to [LICENSE.txt](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/blob/main/LICENSE.txt)
2. See the terms `You are licensed to use compiled versions of Focalboard produced by Mattermost, Inc. under an MIT LICENSE`
3. MIT is a **source code** license specifically granting permission to "modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software". You are using it on specific binaries (that I cannot "modify, merge, ..." without making them *other* binaries)
4. None of the MIT license terms mean anything in this context. The rest of the MIT license is about the copyright notice text, which is not included in your binary (how can text be included in binaries?)

## Expected behavior

Your licensing terms let me know how your software is licensed.

## Additional context

This is like selling someone a toaster and telling them they have to abide by US driving laws when using it. What does it mean? Do I have to use turn-signals before removing toast? Do I need a state-issued ID to operate it? I can't even consider using it.

**If you mean to say that I can use those binaries unmodified for any purpose, just say that. Don't tell me I can use them under the terms of the Civil Aviation Law rules, none of which apply to your tool.**
title: Bug: license terms

body:
## Summary

linux-app should use `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and `$HOME/.config` to save everything that requires write access.

## How important this is to me and why

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. Distribute as a package
2. Per-user config and database

## Additional context/similar features

I've spent some time working on packaging focalboard into the following formats: rpm, deb, flatpak
While everything is going as planned there's a major issue; apps don't have write access to their installed location by default (in focalboard's case, `/opt/focalboard-app`).

While there might be workarounds, the best practice would be to move anything that requires write access to `.config` (`dbconfig` & `filespath` (?)). Doing so will also allow each user to have their own .db as well as ease backups and restores.

From a look at the source, it seems that `config.json` is being ignored (for the app at least).

Here's some solutions I came up with:

- Use the values from the config.json in combination with the hardcoded ones.
- Use cli flags or env vars to override them
- Change the hardcoded value to use `~/.config`
```go
// linux/main.go
...
func runServer(port int) (*server.Server, error) {
	logger, _ := mlog.NewLogger()
+	configFolder := os.Getenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME")
+	if len(configFolder) == 0 {
+		configFolder = os.Getenv("HOME") + "/.config"
+	}

+	configFolder = configFolder + "/focalboard-app"

+	if _, err := os.Stat(configFolder); os.IsNotExist(err) {
+		_ = os.Mkdir(configFolder, 0755)
+	}

	config := &config.Configuration{
		ServerRoot:              fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", port),
		Port:                    port,
		DBType:                  "sqlite3",
+		DBConfigString:          configFolder + "/focalboard.db",
		UseSSL:                  false,
		SecureCookie:            true,
		WebPath:                 "./pack",
		FilesDriver:             "local",
+		FilesPath:               configFolder + "/focalboard_files",
		Telemetry:               true,
		WebhookUpdate:           []string{},
		SessionExpireTime:       259200000000,
		SessionRefreshTime:      18000,
		LocalOnly:               false,
		EnableLocalMode:         false,
		LocalModeSocketLocation: "",
		AuthMode:                "native",
	}
...
```

Some notes on them:
- The third one could be used with #1018 which would handle the creation of the config folder
- #1256 (for linux-app) would also be useful for per-user configuration (plus wouldn't require root to edit it)
- The third solution would unfortunately break existing installations as the hardcoded path would change
- Both the first and second solutions are easy to use with the packages

After a solution to this is found, I'll be able to continue working on packaging focalboard in more formats (rpm, deb, flatpak, appimage, snap).
title: Feature Idea: linux-app should use ~/.config for anything that needs write access

body:
Nice work Boards team on the v0.9.1 enhancements! Posting here to share a few thoughts on further improvements you may or may not have already considered.

1. Welcome to Boards screen seems like it could be scaled up quite significantly

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539472-f305dc5f-d48d-4655-a0e0-0877b8d7a455.png)

2. With no CTA button on the empty screen for a workspace I kind of feel lost with what to do and where I am when I open the board for an empty channel. I'd propose just to duplicate the "+ Add Board" button as a CTA on the empty screen and maybe add an illustration to make it feel friendly and more inviting with a clear action to take (see example proposal). Especially on big monitors the add boards button is reeeally out of the way (see pic). 

Example of empty state with large screen:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539919-9a420bbb-c40b-4769-b948-3ee187ac0a37.png)

Proposal (rough wireframe):
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540100-ff628aca-3819-46c7-955e-1fdc99f48f8a.png)

3. If boards do exist in a channel it would REALLY improve UX if we just opened the first board in the sidebar rather than showed a white screen with instructions to select a board

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135539877-329ffae1-4cd5-4c16-92c8-259aadb1d813.png)

4. Not sure what the effort involved, but sorting the boards in the dashboard and switcher by recently viewed would make both these features significantly more useful. Typing to search is nice, but if I can just see what I'm looking for at a glance it would be better. I bet it's unlikely that users would be actively working in more than a few boards, so surfacing the most active ones would be a big UX win

5. The dashboard view doesn't seem to scroll if there's a lot of channels with boards in them. If the dashboard instead became a "Recently viewed" list with a max number (ie 10), you might not have to support scrolling at all. I know there are some more improvements to the dashboard coming, so just sharing some ideas. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14333569/135540874-3f103b41-a825-4559-8aeb-afbe354df08d.png)

6. I was confused to see the dashboard view had tiles for channels with 0 boards in them. Should we just hide those tiles?

7. I found the reference to "change channels" near the switcher and "All Channels" in the dashboard to be confusing. I guess we are purposely trying to make the connection between boards and channels by doing this.

8. Tiles in the dashboard view did not have an elevation or hover effect
title: FTUE boards UX enhancements

This bug is easily observed on _mysql.test_ but also occurred on https://boards-bugbash.test.mattermost.cloud/

body:
**Summary:**
If I add last updated by to an existing card, I am probably doing it because I want to see when the card was last updated.
However, adding that field causes the last updated date/time to reset to when the property was added.
Having this change not effect last update date/time would allow the user to view when a card was last updated at any point rather than having to have the property already exist.

Importance: Low - Nice to have but not essential


**Additional context / similar features:**

IMO - these fields shouldn't be properties in general, or should be baked in fields somewhere ("view card details in the ... menu?") as well as being properties.  I'd personally prefer they were baked into the card interface because generally these are useful things to know and are related to auditing - not assignment or part of my workflow.
title: Feature Idea: Adding "Last Edited By" Property should not update last edited by field.

When choosing Select or Multi-select, it's not clear how to populate the fields, or whether it's auto-populated, and with what. 

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

Use cases:
1. It's not clear what's being selected or what can be selected - whether it's user-generated or pulled in from somewhere.
2. The field boundaries aren't clear and it's not obvious that the user should type into the field to generate the selection options. There's no cursor to indicate where to type.
3. The name of the field could probably be more helpful. I'm not entirely sure what each field does - LMK and I'm happy to work on some suggestions for renaming.

**Additional context / similar features:**
title: Feature Idea: Select and multi-select fields should be more obvious

body:
**Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Create a new board 2. Add a description 3. Within the description, insert a large image using a Markdown expression like `![astrofish](https://media.snipettemag.com/seance-uploads/2021/8/ocean-genes/1vobi2tLbk2oQknaeBF3HGQ.jpeg)` 4. The image now spills out of the screen because its dimensions are larger than the screen width. Additionally, one can't scroll down to see the cards below, because not enough space is reserved for scrolling down that far. **Expected behavior:** The maximum width for images should be restricted, as happens for images *within* a Markdown card. Also, enough space should be reserved on the bottom so one can scroll down to the bottom of the page. (Right now, the page height seems to be getting calculated without taking the image height into account).
Screenshots (click to expand) Board containing oversized image: ![Board containing oversized image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374789-eb00c6cc-8879-440b-964b-04846d9e0d1c.png) Scroll restriction: I am not able to scroll the page beyond this point to see the table and cards below: ![Board scrolled down as low as possible, but cards still not visible](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374850-4a9df373-f5bc-4cf8-bd34-5b2b771a94b8.png) :point_up: can't scroll more beyond this :( For comparison, here is the board without the card: ![Working board without card](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133374941-dcd49dc6-79b7-46bc-a2f6-bdced63546d0.png) Inline images work fine when inside a card (as opposed to in the main board description): ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view from beginning)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375023-5c601b5d-9124-4c1a-8feb-0b510a25f3ee.png) ![Image embedded in card and working fine (view after scrolling down)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7288084/133375031-3a912d12-b86a-4709-9f25-ca6ca4a59178.png)
**Platform:** - Browser and version: Firefox 91.0.2 - OS and version: Manjaro 21.1.2 "Pahvo" - Focalboard version: Mattermost plugin, v0.8.2 **Additional context:** This seems to be something that can be solved by adjusting the image class. For instance, simply adding the CSS rule ```css img { max-width: 100%; } ``` got the image back down to a reasonable size, although I still wasn't able to scroll down enough to reach the bottom of the page. The images within a card uses a rule for `.octo-block img`, which I'm guessing images in the board description are missing because they aren't part of an `octo-block`. Expanding the full rule to include board description cards makes things even better, but I am *still* not able to scroll down, so that's the main issue to be investigated. PS: If someone were to give me pointers, I'd be happy to have a go at debugging this myself :slightly_smiling_face:
title: Bug: Inline images in board description overflow from screen

body:
**Summary:**
When running as a plugin, Boards should use the Mattermost language setting if none is currently set and that language translation is available. It should fall-back to English as the default if the language is not available.

**Additional context / similar features:**
For now, the user can choose a language in Boards independent of Mattermost, but Boards should default to the Mattermost language setting for consistency.

Also, to clarify the expected behavior:
* If a user select a language (e.g. Spanish) in Mattermost Channels then switches to Boards for the first time, the interface should be in that language (Spanish).
  * Unless the translation is not available, in which case default to English.
* If the user chooses a different language in Boards (e.g. Indonesian), Boards will display in that language, but this will not affect the Channels setting
  * From this point on, Boards will show in that explicit language setting (Indonesian) regardless of the Mattermost setting
* Channels will always show the language in the Mattermost setting
title: Honor the Mattermost language setting if possible

body:
**Summary:**

The Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 on my Mattermost Teams Edition 5.38.0 installed with docker behind an Apache2 reverse proxy installation has websocket connection interruptions.
Mattermost works perfectly without any issue, chat is updated live and have been working fine since the beginning.
Focalboard works but does not save everything, probably due to the websocket issue.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**

1. Install Focalboard plugin v0.8.2 via MarketPlace
2. Update the apache configuration following the documentation [here](https://www.focalboard.com/download/mattermost/#with-apache-unofficial)
3. Activate it
4. Clic on Focalboard icon in a channel

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should not show the banner about websocket connection loss.

**Screenshots:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25644865/131575838-6b2346fe-e745-44a7-96c5-c2ccb0bac946.png)

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: Chrome 92
 - Mattermost Server v5.38.0 (docker)
 - Mattermost Desktop 4.7.1
 - Focalboard plugin v0.8.2
 - OS and version: macOS 11.5.2

**Additional context:**

Here is the relevant part of my Apache2 configuration:
```apache
    RewriteEngine On
    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} /api/v[0-9]+/(users/)?websocket|/plugins/focalboard/ws/* [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:UPGRADE} ^WebSocket$ [NC,OR]
    RewriteCond %{HTTP:CONNECTION} ^Upgrade$ [NC]
    RewriteRule .* ws://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/plugins/focalboard
    RewriteRule ^ http://%{HTTP_HOST}/mattermost%{REQUEST_URI} [R=301]

    RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} ^/mattermost
    RewriteRule .* http://127.0.0.1:8065%{REQUEST_URI} [P,QSA,L]

    <Location /mattermost>
            Require all granted
            ProxyPass http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverse http://127.0.0.1:8065/
            ProxyPassReverseCookieDomain 127.0.0.1 dsimb.inserm.fr
    </Location>
```
title: Bug: plugin websocket connection interrupted behind Apache2 reverse proxy

**How important this is to me and why:**

Could also be the ability to fold/unfold groups/workspaces in a nested hierarchy in the menubar.

Example from another project tool, that has the name of the first day of the work week.

body:
### Summary
Please allow us to add userscripts and userstyles to the DOM.

### How important this is to me and why:
Importance: Medium

### Use cases:
There are loads of feature requests and bugs in the issue tracker. Obviously there are many great ideas but not enough time to implement them all.

Some apps allow you to use custom userscripts (using tampermonkey, for example) and custom userstyles (using stylish, for example) to mod the interface. I expect that would lead to less feature requests for little UI tweaks and thus allow devs to focus on the important stuff.

For example, I added a [feature request](https://github.com/mattermost/focalboard/issues/1055) to rename the "NO STATUS" column. I expect many don't care for that, so it may take forever to be implemented, if ever. But if I could use a userscript, I'd just find that node in the DOM and either delete it or rename it - I wouldn't bother asking for it as a formal feature.

### Implementation
The easiest way to implement this is to allow for `custom.js` and `custom.css` files that are appended to the DOM at the very end of the loading process. They could be placed in the same location as `/opt/focalboard/config.json`.

**So if `/opt/focalboard/custom.js` and `/opt/focalboard/custom.css` are found, they're appended to the DOM, else not.**

### Additional context
If this is implemented, we could share our useful js/css in a github gist, until they make their way into the actual code.

Note that custom js/css can be used right now, but only for you - it wouldn't work for focalboard's other users. Hence this feature request.
title: Feature Idea: Custom css and js

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to be able to filter by dates, numbers, regex patterns on strings / urls / etc.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

To be able to use focalboard to store data, filtering is a key requirement for me. For example if I want to track tasks, having due dates on those tasks, being able to build a view that shows only tasks that are overdue seems crucial.

Use cases:
1. Show only tasks overdue
2. Show only tasks that can be started after X date
3. Add a numerical priority field and show only tasks with prio > 3

**Additional context / similar features:**
Trello has filtering capabilities of some kind, I'm not familiar with asana / notion but I'd assume they do.

FYI, if this is already implemented, then I'm reporting a bug that on the standalone desktop app on macOS I can only seem to filter by status fields and not others.
title: Feature Idea: Filter by more field types

body:
**Summary:**
I'd like to have an overview of all my projects on a single board. Then I'd like to be able to click from that board directly to another board for each project. This would be possible in the web based version using simple URLs, but not in the desktop version.

In the spirit of mattermost/mattermost-server#23273 it would be cool to have a property type which contains a link to another board. Both as a choice (so I can choose one of my existing boards as a dropdown value) and as a "link" which allows me to click to that board.

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Low

Use cases:
1. Maintaining an overview board that shows an overview of individual projects, each which have their own board
2. Being able to take 1 list of tasks and break it out into a list of boards (so each task gets a whole board)
3. Maintaining a list of persons assigned to boards by having a meta board that shows the person and the board per card
4. Adding metadata to boards by having a single overview board that tracks boards and extra metadata
5. Allowing for custom "navigation" scenarios, where users can be sent to a single board to start with and from there find their way to other boards

**Additional context / similar features:**
Not sure if this is implemented elsewhere or not.
title: Feature Idea: Property type that is a link to another board

body:
**Summary:**
If you have several different teams using different themes, this isn't reflected in focalboard which will only use one theme regardless of the team it's being accessed from.
This is only a problem when accessing from the channel header icon, accessing from the app switcher applies the theme for every team.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Install focalboard on mattermost
2. Open a team with a dark theme
3. Open focalboard from that team - focalboard has the dark theme applied
4. Switch to a team with the default light theme and open focalboard from that team using the channel header icon - focalboard again has the dark theme applied

**Expected behavior:**
Focalboard should use the default light theme (blue/white)
title: Bug: Focalboard doesn't apply different themes per team when opened from the channel header

Docker build is failing because build context is wrong.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**

2. `sudo docker build -t focalboard .`

Here you can see the error because the build context is wrong.

body:
**Summary:**
The plugin for the Mattermost app currently opens as a "separate window" to mattermost, however this is not selectable from the windows taskbar. Instead, you have to open the mattermost application and then the focalboard window pops up on top.

**How important this is to me and why:**
If I've got a project that I'm working on, it would be nice to have the board for that project available all the time, so that I can make focus directly on that board instead of having to open mattermost, then focus on the board.

It would also be useful to be able to switch between channels within the focalboard window.


Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. User has a project that they want to focus on without being distracted by the mattermost window in the background
2. User wants to go directly to the focalboard project instead of opening Mattermost, then focalboard.
3. User does not want to have focalboard project appear in the way of their mattermost window when they return to mattermost.

**Additional context / similar features:**
good implementation / workaround of this capability: Using mattermost from a browser. You can open the focalboard plugin in a separate tab, put it in to a separate window, etc.
title: Feature Idea: Enable plugin as a separate window to Mattermost

body:
**Summary:**
Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Open a card.
2. Add some items to it such as comments or text. Notice that adding these will update the card's updated at timestamp.
3. Now delete some added comments. Notice that doing so brings back the previous updated at for the card.

**Expected behavior:**
Deleting a card component should update car's updated at timestamp to the moment something was deleted.

**Screenshots:**
NA

**Platform:**
 - Browser and version: all browsers
 - OS and version: all OS

**Additional context:**
Card's updated at is calculated from the most recently updated component in the client. Since deleted components are not sent in the blocks API, the client is not aware of their existence. Hence, the outdated updated at time.
title: Bug: Deleting a card component doesn't update the card's updated at timestamp

body:
**Summary:**
Copy a card from one board to another or even better to sync the card to another board, so you can have it on two boards simultaneously and only need to update it in one place.  

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. OKRs/Roadmaps: PMs or Eng can have their own roadmap/OKR for their area of ownership and sync a card to a combined board
2. Release management: We can have a focalboard to track all features that are getting into a release from roadmaps. 
3. Mulitple teams working on similar items: (Potentially could be used as an epic once we can do task linking) Task for tracking design, then a task for implementation by the development team, then a task for docs all on their own focalboards. 
4. Personal task management: Getting a task card from a project board to my own task tracking board for personal time allocation. 

**Additional context / similar features:**
Productboard will do a sync with Jira tasks, so I am loosely basing off of that functionality.
title: Feature Idea: Copy or Sync card to another board

**Steps to reproduce the behavio*u*r:**
1. Get logged out of Focalboard due to an expired session

Be able to assign a card to a user. There are several ways we could implement this:
1. Add an "assigned to" property to each card (so every card has this)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: High

1. Show task assignees for projects
2. Notify assignees of changes

**Additional context / similar features:**
A related feature is adding a "due date" property, so that assignees can be notified of pending deadlines.

This is related to the [user property type feature](https://focalboard-community.octo.mattermost.com/workspace/qgsck6cts3fwpqwyjiupjm5cde/shared?id=47aa9bb4-6967-4a96-83c7-11bd6b20f1eb&v=ca1a5441-4c1c-4d0e-856e-88cc744576c8&r=762db9c6-305c-4d13-8ce6-9f11e5232c24&c=2b8cc90a-e91c-4d62-8d95-2f219ab248a7).

body:
**Summary:**
Focalboard should also have a timeline / roadmap

**How important is this::**
It's tough to define the importance but let me put it this way...
everyone else you compare yourself to, has it (sometimes with third-party tools).

On the Focalboard website the catch is "Focalboard is an open source, self-hosted alternative to Trello, Notion, and Asana."
Since I tried Trello and Asana within the last months I wanted to test focalboard next. Sadly the timeline / gantt / roadmap is an essential feature (for me). Since there is none, I just can't jump in and use it.

Importance: Medium / High

Use cases:
1. Timeline view (Gantt chart)
2. Project roadmap / status
3. "project planning" in general?

**Additional context / similar features:**
I didn't try Notion but they have one too apparently: [https://www.notion.vip/meet-notions-timeline-view/](https://www.notion.vip/meet-notions-timeline-view/)
TeamGantt for Trello looks stunning: [https://www.teamgantt.com](https://www.teamgantt.com)
Asana has a great timeline implemented: [https://asana.com/product/timeline](https://asana.com/product/timeline)
title: Feature Idea: Timeline / Gantt

body:
**Summary:**
Each card can have an auto incrementing number similar to issues in Github/Jira in order to better reference cards when developing software (in Commits or MRs/PRs)

**How important this is to me and why:**

Importance: Medium

Use cases:
1. Software development
2. Talking about/referencing issues within Mattermost or in Meetings

**Additional context / similar features:**
- Jira does this with adding project ID and a auto-incrementing number before each ticket
 Example: `https://jira.example.com/browse/OWSWS-422` -> ID = `OWSWS-422`
- Trello has this at the beginning of the unique identifier in the URL
 Example: `https://trello.com/c/[boardid]/5893-weddingvideo-n2` -> ID = `5893`
title: Feature Idea: Show ID/Number on each card
